description:
Source Reference: pt-ant
Status: open
Priority: P1
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex,claude
Tags: nextjs,index,ios,dropdown,refactor,ui
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/components/SessionLoggerModal.js
- pt-rebuild/components/ExercisePicker.js
- pt-rebuild/lib/session-form-params.js
- pt-rebuild/components/

Issue:
Extract the working `/index` dropdown behavior into a shared reusable select UI component/module in `components/` that other pages can import.

Context:
`/index` is the current proof point because `SessionLoggerModal` already supports both plain native selects and an `Other...` path for form parameters. This issue is not just to copy that structure elsewhere. It is to turn the proven index behavior into a reusable implementation that centralizes the JSX/CSS/interaction rules for iPhone-safe selects.

Required Output:
- A shared reusable component/module in `components/`
- It must support:
  - plain native select fields
  - native select plus `Other...` fallback text entry
- `Other...` must be configurable so a call site can explicitly allow or disallow it.
- It may use small supporting pure helpers, but the reuse must be at the UI-component/module level, not just helper functions.

Constraints/Caveats:
Keep the behavior native-select-first. Preserve current working index behavior while extracting it. Shared implementation must be suitable for adoption on `/program` and `/pt-view`.

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Current `/index` dropdown behavior is preserved
- [ ] Shared reusable select component/module exists in `components/`
- [ ] `Other...` fallback remains supported through the shared implementation
- [ ] `Other...` can be enabled or disabled per use site

Dependencies:
- parent-child: pt-ant

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a
title: Extract reusable iPhone-safe select pattern from /index

Status: open

Issue:
Next.js tracker uses an inline successBanner div instead of the static app's fixed floating toast overlay. The static app has a position:fixed toast (bottom: 2rem, slides up from translateY(100px), opacity transition 0.3s) with success (green) and error (red) variants. The Next.js version must match this behavior.

Context:
Static app: #toastContainer div with showToast(message, type, duration). Types: default, error, success. Used for Saved (with notes/no notes), offline fallback, sync status, error feedback.
Next.js current: successBanner div in index.module.css — inline, no position:fixed, no animation. This is a placeholder and needs to be replaced with a proper toast component.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Toast must be position:fixed so it overlays content (does not push layout)
- Slides up from bottom on show (translateY transition)
- Auto-dismisses after timeout (3s default)
- success and error variants required (matches static CSS vars)
- iOS safe area aware (bottom position must account for safe-area-inset-bottom)

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Toast appears floating (position:fixed, overlays content)
- [ ] Slides up from bottom on show, fades out on dismiss
- [ ] 'Saved (with notes)' and 'Saved (no notes)' shown after save
- [ ] Error variant renders in red
- [ ] iOS safe area respected at bottom
- [ ] successBanner inline div removed from index.js

Dependencies:
- none

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a
title: Implement floating toast component for Next.js tracker

description:

Issue:
Form parameters (e.g. band_resistance) render as plain text inputs in Next.js SessionLoggerModal. Static index.html renders them as smart dropdowns built from historical log values, with an 'Other...' fallback to free text.

File scope:
pt-rebuild/components/SessionLoggerModal.js, pt-rebuild/pages/index.js, pt-rebuild/hooks/useSessionLogging.js, pt-rebuild/lib/session-form-params.js

Context:
Discovered during DN-048 regression testing (2026-03-03). Codex implemented parity code updates: smart dropdown + 'Other...' for non-unit params, history-driven defaults for create flow (including value+unit parsing like `3 lb`), timezone-safe datetime-local rendering, numeric weight/distance inputs, and select placeholder parity when default exists. This is a DN-048 sub-fix and closes with DN-048 after DN-058 verification passes.

Constraints/Caveats:
No onClick. onPointerUp only. 44px targets. Keep existing weight/distance unit-selector behavior unchanged. 'Other...' custom mode needs per-set-per-param local state in SessionLoggerModal (useState). Pre-existing form_data values (edit flow) that aren't in historicalFormParams must still display — add them as an option or pre-select correctly. Verification is tracked in DN-058.
notes:
2026-03-13 QA review: original plain-text-vs-dropdown gap looks mostly implemented. Remaining parity issues are narrower: (1) static form-parameter defaults/history are side-aware for sided exercises via showLogSetModal/showNextSetModal -> renderFormParameterFields(... side) -> getLastUsedParamValue/getHistoricalParamValues, but Next.js helpers are side-agnostic; (2) static getLastUsedParamValue checks currentSession before persisted history, while Next.js seeds default_form_data from logs at exercise-select time and reuses that snapshot for panel/manual/next-set flows. See children ptrebuild-1qq.1 and ptrebuild-1qq.2 for source-backed follow-ups.
2026-03-13 stop-point handoff: Preview verification on https://pt-rehab-git-nextjs-pt-tracker.vercel.app shows the original DN-056 gap is mostly resolved. Verified on test-patient account: (1) create flow for Ankle Dorsiflexion (Theraband) now reopens with smart dropdown prefilled to last-used value 'black' instead of empty placeholder; (2) 'Other...' fallback renders correctly and preserves the current value in a text box; (3) panel-seeded Next Set confirmation now carries form_data and shows Parameters: band resistance / black. Remaining open scope is now limited to the two new child bugs: ptrebuild-1qq.1 (in-session last-used form_data parity) and ptrebuild-1qq.2 (side-aware form-parameter defaults/history parity). Implementation should stay single-threaded because both children converge in pages/index.js and lib/session-form-params.js. Safe parallel support next session: separate thread does review-only or preview verification after code lands; do not split implementation of .1 and .2 across threads.
2026-03-21 child parity follow-ups are now complete and preview-verified on https://pt-rehab-git-nextjs-pt-tracker.vercel.app. Using the live patient account on Ankle Dorsiflexion (Theraband), Log Set reopened with the current-session right-side value (blue) without reload, and switching Side to Left immediately restored the distinct left-side value (black). No remaining unique scope is left under DN-056 beyond the already-closed original smart-dropdown/'Other...' parity work.
title: [DN-056] Form parameters (e.g. band_resistance) render as plain text inputs in Next.js SessionLoggerModal. Static index.html renders them as smart dropdowns built from historical log values, with an 'Other...' fallback to free text.

close_reason: Implemented and preview-verified on 2026-03-21
description: Static getLastUsedParamValue(exerciseId,paramName,side) checks currentSession before persisted history, and showLogSetModal/showNextSetModal call renderFormParameterFields each time the modal opens. Current Next.js computes default_form_data from logs only when the exercise is selected and then reuses that snapshot for handleTimerApplySet and handleTimerOpenManual. SessionLoggerModal historicalFormParams also comes from collectGlobalParameterValues(logs), which excludes current in-session sets until reload. Result: after a user changes a form parameter earlier in the same session, later create/next-set/panel-seeded logs can keep stale defaults/options instead of the latest in-session value.
notes:
2026-03-13 stop-point: not implemented yet in this thread. Evidence from code + review: Next.js still seeds default_form_data once at exercise select and derives historical options from persisted logs, so same-session changes can remain stale until reload. Expected implementation surface next session: pages/index.js handoff/state + lib/session-form-params.js helpers, with possible SessionLoggerModal wiring if current-set values need to refresh options live. Do not assign this as a separate implementation thread from ptrebuild-1qq.2 because both touch the same files.
2026-03-21 implementation in progress: session-form-params helper layer now prefers current in-session accepted sets before persisted history, and tracker/manual wiring now requests fresh form-parameter defaults/options from live tracker state instead of reusing the exercise-select snapshot. Runtime verification still pending.
2026-03-21 preview verification on https://pt-rehab-git-nextjs-pt-tracker.vercel.app using the live patient account confirms in-session last-used parity. On Ankle Dorsiflexion (Theraband), Log Set reopened with Resistance Band prefilled to blue immediately after saving a draft right-side set in the same session, without requiring a page reload or server save.
title: [DN-056] In-session last-used form_data parity is still missing for panel-seeded/create flows

close_reason: Implemented and preview-verified on 2026-03-21
description: Static index form-parameter helpers accept and apply side for sided exercises: showLogSetModal/renderFormParameterFields pass the current side, getHistoricalParamValues(exerciseId,paramName,side) filters by side, and getLastUsedParamValue(exerciseId,paramName,side) filters currentSession/history by side. Current Next.js form-parameter helpers are side-agnostic: collectGlobalParameterValues(logs) is global-only, getLastUsedParameterDisplay(logs,exerciseId,paramName) has no side input, buildDefaultFormDataForExercise(exercise,logs) has no side input, pages/index.js seeds default_form_data once per selected exercise, and SessionLoggerModal renders historicalFormParams[paramName] without recomputing when set.side changes. Result: left/right sided logging can prefill or offer the wrong form_data values.
notes:
2026-03-13 stop-point: not implemented yet in this thread. Evidence from static review: public/index.html passes side into form-parameter history/default helpers, while current Next.js helpers remain side-agnostic. Expected implementation surface next session: lib/session-form-params.js for side-aware history/default lookup, pages/index.js for side-aware seeding in panel/manual/next-set flows, and possibly SessionLoggerModal if per-set side changes must refresh options/defaults. Do not split this into a separate implementation thread from ptrebuild-1qq.1 because both converge in the same helper/state path.
2026-03-21 implementation in progress: session-form-params helper layer now supports side-aware history/default lookup, SessionLoggerModal now reads side-scoped option lists for sided exercises, and manual/next-set tracker flows rebuild form-parameter context when side changes. Runtime verification still pending.
2026-03-21 preview verification on https://pt-rehab-git-nextjs-pt-tracker.vercel.app using the live patient account confirms side-aware parity. In the same draft session for Ankle Dorsiflexion (Theraband), Right reopened with Resistance Band = blue, switching the Side field to Left immediately changed the default to black, and reopening again preserved the distinct left/right values.
title: [DN-056] Side-aware form-parameter defaults/history parity is still missing in Next.js logging flow

close_reason: Completed
description:
Source Reference: discovered during offline cache root cause analysis 2026-03-13
Status: open
Priority: P1
Risk: high
Scope: Next.js PWA service worker (Phase 4h)
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: yes
Tags: offline, pwa, service-worker, cache, nextjs, migration
File Scope: pt-rebuild/public/sw.js (new Next.js sw, not static app sw.js)

Issue:
The Next.js service worker must pre-cache root URL / not /index.html. If it pre-caches /index.html (as static app does), offline cold-open fails — browser navigates to / and caches.match() finds no entry. User sees 'Offline - no cached version available' and no JS runs — IndexedDB read cache never reached.

Root Cause (confirmed in static app 2026-03-13):
Static sw.js STATIC_ASSETS includes '/index.html'. Browser navigate requests are for the root URL '/'. Different cache keys. network-first cache.put() stores root URL dynamically but does not persist reliably on iOS between sessions.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Pre-cache '/' in STATIC_ASSETS, not '/index.html'
- Navigate fallback handler must try caches.match('/') before returning offline error
- Do NOT repeat the /index.html pattern from static app sw.js
- This must be resolved before ptrebuild-zb3 (IndexedDB read cache) can work — if page does not load, cache code never runs

Validation Checklist:
- Open app online, close, airplane mode, reopen: app shell loads from SW cache
- Exercises load from IndexedDB (ptrebuild-zb3)
- No 'Offline - no cached version available' on cold-open after prior online visit

Dependencies: discovered-from ptrebuild-zb3, child of ptrebuild-v3l, blocks ptrebuild-zb3
notes:
2026-03-21 runtime investigation with Chrome DevTools found the service worker install path was still failing on preview because /index.html returned 404 on the Next.js route surface while sw.js still included it in STATIC_ASSETS. That leaves the new worker stuck installing with an empty cache and reproduces offline cold-open failure (ERR_INTERNET_DISCONNECTED) even though '/' is listed. Implemented fix: removed /index.html from STATIC_ASSETS, mapped /index.html navigation fallback to '/', and bumped CACHE_NAME to pt-tracker-v13 so the corrected precache can install cleanly. Local verification: npm run build passed.
2026-03-21 runtime verification on the nextjs preview after cache clear: manually unregistered the old worker, deleted Cache Storage entries, reloaded online, and confirmed a clean pt-tracker-v13 install with 17 cached assets and active controller state. Then switched DevTools to Offline and reloaded /. Result: the root route stayed on https://pt-rehab-git-nextjs-pt-tracker.vercel.app/ and rendered the PT Tracker Sign In shell instead of chrome-error / ERR_INTERNET_DISCONNECTED. This verifies the page-shell cold-open failure was caused by the stale /index.html precache entry and is resolved by the sw.js fix. Remaining read-side data bootstrap validation still belongs to pt-zb3 / pt-780.1.
2026-03-21 verified fixed on preview after cache clear: active worker pt-tracker-v13 installed cleanly and offline reload of / rendered the app shell instead of chrome-error. Service-worker fix commit 5cc17ce is already pushed to origin/nextjs.
title: Next.js service worker must pre-cache root URL / not /index.html for offline cold-open

Issue:
Establish and apply process rules governing all parity verification work for the Next.js migration.

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/legacy-parity-governance/spec.md
The governance spec defines how parity work must be conducted: the static tracker baseline is the source of truth, canonical specs are the primary behavioral reference, named specifics (copy strings, field names, helper names, localStorage keys) must survive carry-forward verbatim, and Beads are the execution layer created only after specs are stable. The spec also requires that suspected static inconsistencies be surfaced explicitly rather than silently normalized.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code
- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec
- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass
- Do not introduce new behavior not described in the spec

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] All child parity issues (p2 through p5 and their children) are closed
- [ ] No open parity gaps exist across any of the five parity domains
- [ ] Named specifics (copy strings, helper names, keys, endpoint signatures) were verified verbatim, not summarized
- [ ] All suspected static inconsistencies were explicitly surfaced as review items rather than silently normalized
- [ ] Runtime testing was used to validate documented behavior, not to discover it for the first time
- [ ] Each migrated tracker slice was compared against the documented static contract before any runtime walkthrough

Source Reference: migration overlap review on 2026-03-17
Status: open
Priority: P1
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: nextjs,migration,parity,governance,beads
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/.beads/
- pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/tasks.md

Issue:
Review the active Next.js migration Beads and add cross-references anywhere a parity Bead and an implementation/refactor Bead overlap in behavior surface but differ in purpose.

Context:
The migration is partially complete, so the tracker currently contains both spec-derived parity verification Beads and older implementation/refactor Beads. Some parity Beads will require code changes, others are verification-only, and some shared implementation Beads support multiple parity tasks. We need explicit cross-references so agents do not duplicate work or miss a dependency while the migration is in flight.

Required Direction:
- Add related links for overlapping behavior surfaces first.
- Keep parity Beads as the acceptance surface.
- Keep shared implementation/refactor Beads separate when they support multiple parity tasks.
- Do not merge or supersede unless the overlap is clearly same-purpose and one issue no longer adds independent value.

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Existing overlapping parity and implementation/refactor Beads are identified
- [ ] Appropriate related links are added for same-behavior/different-purpose overlaps
- [ ] No merge/supersede is done unless overlap is truly redundant
- [ ] Follow-up prune candidates are noted where overlap may later be consolidated

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: pt-477

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a
title: Cross-reference overlapping parity and implementation Beads

close_reason: Completed
description:
Source Reference: discovered during Playwright auth-storage verification on 2026-03-21
Status: in_progress
Priority: P1
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: nextjs,auth,offline,indexeddb,localstorage,investigation,agent:codex
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/lib/supabase.js
- pt-rebuild/lib/offline-cache.js
- pt-rebuild/node_modules/@supabase/*

Issue:
During isolated Playwright verification, the browser context exposed a localStorage entry named sb-zvgoaxdpkgfxklotqwpz-auth-token even though the Next.js Supabase client is intended to persist auth via IndexedDB through authStorage. Investigate whether any active Next.js auth path still writes to localStorage, or whether the observed key is created by Supabase internals for another reason.

Context:
Current code in lib/supabase.js passes auth.storage = authStorage from lib/offline-cache.js. However, browser inspection still surfaced the default Supabase auth key name in localStorage. We need to determine whether this is an actual persistence regression, an auxiliary mirror key from Supabase internals, or a non-app artifact, and fix it if the active Next.js path still relies on localStorage.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Use code inspection and runtime evidence; do not assume the source of the key
- Do not break warmed-session offline restore while investigating storage behavior
- Preserve the existing IndexedDB-backed auth path unless runtime evidence shows a real gap
- Update README if ownership or persistence behavior changes

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Source of the localStorage auth key is identified
- [ ] Confirm whether active Next.js auth writes still reach localStorage
- [ ] If needed, active auth persistence is constrained to the intended IndexedDB path
- [ ] Any remaining localStorage usage is documented as intentional legacy or removed

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: pt-780.10
- blocks: n/a

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a
notes: 2026-03-21 investigation result: local package inspection shows Supabase auth-js uses the provided auth.storage directly and only falls back to globalThis.localStorage when no custom storage is passed. Our lib/supabase.js still passes authStorage from lib/offline-cache.js, so the intended Next.js auth path remains IndexedDB-backed. Runtime verification in Playwright: after clearing localStorage and deleting IndexedDB databases, reloading the Next.js sign-in page did not recreate sb-zvgoaxdpkgfxklotqwpz-auth-token in localStorage, while the shared IndexedDB database pt_rebuild_offline was created. This indicates the observed localStorage key was preexisting browser state in the automation context, not an active page-load write from the current Next.js auth flow.
title: Investigate unexpected Supabase auth key presence in localStorage

close_reason: Implemented: hooks/useUserContext.js created, usePtViewData refactored, both pages updated. Commit c302242.
description:
MessagesModal needs viewerName/otherName/otherIsTherapist/profileId/recipientId on every page that uses messaging. Currently index.js fetches users locally and derives these fields; pt-view.js is missing them entirely. Any future page adding messaging would duplicate this logic again.

Fix: create hooks/useUserContext.js as the single reusable source of truth for user identity and messaging context. Refactor usePtViewData to accept { token, patientId } instead of session (eliminating its internal fetchUsers call). Both index.js and pt-view.js call useUserContext(session) and pass messaging fields to MessagesModal.

Files:
- NEW: hooks/useUserContext.js — fetches users, returns profileId, patientId, recipientId, userRole, emailEnabled, viewerName, otherName, otherIsTherapist, loading, error
- MODIFY: hooks/usePtViewData.js — change signature to ({ token, patientId }), remove fetchUsers call, return only logs/programs/dataError/offlineNotice
- MODIFY: pages/pt-view.js — call useUserContext, pass messaging props to MessagesModal, local emailEnabled state synced from userCtx
- MODIFY: pages/index.js — replace local fetchUsers useEffect with useUserContext call

emailEnabled toggle pattern: pages keep local state initialized from userCtx.emailEnabled, call patchEmailNotifications + local setter on toggle.

Offline cache: useUserContext handles cacheUsers; usePtViewData handles cacheLogs/cachePrograms.
title: Refactor: shared useUserContext hook for messaging identity

description:
Source Reference: implementation-patterns violation sweep 2026-03-20
Status: open

Issue:
Legacy static tracker surfaces still keep important app state in page-local localStorage paths instead of the approved shared offline-storage helpers. This includes the tracker offline queue/sync flow in public/index.html, message/UI state in public/pt_view.html, and editor auth bootstrap storage in public/js/pt_editor.js.

Context:
Discovered during the implementation-patterns violation sweep after IMPLEMENTATION_PATTERNS.md was added. The tracker queue persistence is especially important because the current implementation-patterns binder says app persistence should use the shared IndexedDB-backed/offline-cache path rather than new page-local localStorage ownership.

Required scope:
- Replace or retire remaining page-local tracker queue persistence and sync ownership in public/index.html
- Move remaining pt_view UI/message state storage onto shared offline-cache helpers where appropriate
- Remove editor auth bootstrap localStorage usage in favor of the shared storage path
- Coordinate with existing offline architecture work where these findings overlap broader migration tasks
- Keep IMPLEMENTATION_PATTERNS.md accurate if shared storage guidance changes while resolving this work

Constraints/Caveats:
- This issue overlaps existing offline-architecture work; do not duplicate implementation unnecessarily if another active bead already owns a deeper Next.js-side migration slice
- Preserve legacy static functionality during migration
- Avoid introducing any new localStorage persistence

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] No scoped app-state persistence in the legacy tracker/editor surfaces relies on page-local localStorage ownership where a shared storage helper exists
- [ ] Tracker queue ownership is aligned with the approved shared offline-storage direction
- [ ] pt_view UI/message state persistence is aligned with shared helpers where applicable
- [ ] IMPLEMENTATION_PATTERNS.md remains accurate if storage guidance changes during the fix

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: pt-pbs
- relates-to: pt-780.10

Reactivation Trigger:
Reopen if later migration work leaves additional legacy static storage paths outside the shared offline helpers.

otesCollapsed, lastSentMessageTime,  iltersExpanded all bootstrap from localStorage)\n- pt-rebuild/public/pt_view.html:1521 (
otesCollapsed persisted to localStorage)\n- pt-rebuild/public/pt_view.html:2226,2231 (message timestamps persisted to localStorage)\n- pt-rebuild/public/js/pt_editor.js:60,74 (editor auth bootstrap reads/removes pt_editor_auth from localStorage)\nOverlap note: broader offline-architecture migration is tracked in pt-780.10; this issue is the implementation-pattern/storage-drift slice.

description:
Source Reference: discovered during 2026-03-21 cutover planning discussion
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: shared
Agent Eligible: shared
Tags: nextjs,cutover,legacy,public,cleanup,agent:shared
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/public/
- pt-rebuild/README.md
- pt-rebuild/docs/

Issue:
Determine exactly which legacy static files must remain on the nextjs branch and which should move to the legacy fallback branch/subdomain only, so the branch can eventually be pruned without breaking Next.js deployment.

Context:
The user wants nextjs to become a clean modern branch that can eventually replace main, while the legacy static app remains available on a separate branch and subdomain as fallback. The old static app should not live indefinitely inside nextjs just for parity reference. Legacy code will still exist locally and in git history, so parity comparison does not depend on keeping it in the active nextjs tree.

Current exploration context:
- public currently contains legacy HTML routes, legacy css/js directories, shared icons/manifests, sw.js, and stray temp artifacts.
- Active Next.js references already confirmed include /manifest-tracker.json, /icons/icon.svg, and /sw.js.
- reset-password.html may or may not still be a legitimate nextjs-side asset and needs explicit review.

Deliverable:
A keep/prune map with explicit file lists, rationale, and any required README/doc updates. The output should be concrete enough that a follow-up implementation bead can prune the branch safely.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Do not delete anything yet; this bead is planning/inventory only.
- Assume the fallback legacy branch/subdomain will exist before pruning begins.
- The keep/prune map must preserve Vercel deployability for nextjs.
- Future agents should be able to tell clearly which remaining public/ files are true browser-served assets for Next.js versus legacy leftovers.

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Inventory of legacy/static files is explicit
- [ ] Files needed by Next.js are clearly separated from legacy-only files
- [ ] reset-password.html and any other edge-case files have an explicit keep/prune decision
- [ ] README/doc update targets are identified
- [ ] Output is sufficient to drive a prune implementation bead

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: pt-amc
- blocks: n/a

Reactivation Trigger:
Resume when starting the legacy-prune cutover-prep lane.

Issue:
Complete the split capability specs until implementation and Beads-slicing agents no longer need to open public/index.html or reread design-extract.md to understand or rebuild static tracker behavior.

Context:
This is the main anti-loss step in the chain. The user’s target is a true never-need-to-look-at-index baseline that captures every last feature, button, UX rule, process, flow, API call, state clear/reset, timing rule, and copy detail needed for full migration and reconstruction. The extraction harvest exists as evidence, but agents should assume they will not go back to it during Next.js work; the normative WHAT contract must therefore carry forward every extraction detail that would matter during implementation or Beads slicing.

Constraints/Caveats:
Specs must define WHAT the system does in normative, scenario-based terms. Do not turn the spec into migration design. Do not assume details are safe just because they exist in design-extract or baseline-design; if an implementation agent or Beads author needs them, the spec layer must carry them explicitly. Preserve named values verbatim when they affect parity, including copy strings, field names, helper names, storage keys, mutation identifiers, exact clear/reset targets, API outcomes, and ordered sequences that change visible behavior.

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Split specs are complete enough that an agent can explain and slice static tracker behavior without opening public/index.html
- [ ] Beads can be authored from the canonical spec package without rediscovering behavior in design-extract.md
- [ ] User-visible flows, timing, ordering, role context, save behavior, offline behavior, copy, API interactions, and exact clear/reset behavior are explicit in the specs
- [ ] No migration-critical tracker behavior exists only in design-extract or baseline-design without a usable contract path in the split specs
- [ ] coverage-matrix.md maps every migration-critical extract domain and review unit to an exact canonical home so coverage is proved rather than assumed
- [ ] Runtime testing is no longer required to discover baseline truth; it is only used to validate implementation

- discovered-from: pt-7fy

n/a

Context:
The structure rules allow exemption if both agents agree the file cannot be cleanly split and the hard cap is unavoidable given the domain. index.js is the main tracker shell — it orchestrates auth, data, offline queue, logging, timer, history, and session finalization. Each concern is already extracted to hooks/components. What remains is orchestration glue. If that glue is genuinely one concern (orchestration), an exemption may be valid. Issue:
Verify that offline startup shows correct feedback when cache is present, shows explicit failed-load behavior when cache is absent, and scopes cache fallback to the resolved patient.

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-offline-and-timing-parity/spec.md
When the tracker boots without network or when online loads fail: if suitable cached data exists, fall back to cache and show explicit feedback "Offline mode" or "Using cached data". Cache fallback is only valid when matching cached tracker data exists for the resolved patient context (viewingPatientId must be resolved before attempting cache fallback). If no usable cache exists, preserve explicit failed-load behavior rather than pretending tracker data was recovered. No silent empty states.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code
- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec
- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass
- Do not introduce new behavior not described in the spec

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] "Offline mode" or "Using cached data" is shown when cache fallback is used
- [ ] Cache fallback is only attempted after viewingPatientId is resolved
- [ ] Cache is only used when it contains data for the resolved patient context (not any cached data)
- [ ] Explicit failed-load behavior (not a blank shell) is shown when no usable cache exists
- [ ] "Failed to load exercises. Check your connection." is shown when exercise load fails with no cache

notes:
2026-03-22 Codex reproduction note on current nextjs preview: on a fresh Chrome DevTools page at https://pt-rehab-git-nextjs-pt-tracker.vercel.app/, the active service worker/controller and cache are current (sw.js with CACHE_NAME pt-tracker-v14). Switching DevTools network emulation to Offline and reloading the unauthenticated root page kept the app shell alive and rendered the Sign In surface offline instead of falling through to chrome-error. This suggests the new failing runtime report is narrower than basic route-shell caching: the remaining failure is likely the authenticated tracker-startup path (or a different browser context) rather than a total loss of root SW navigation fallback.
2026-03-22 restart note: a non-isolated Chrome DevTools MCP entry has been added locally so the next Codex session can use a warmed shared browser context after restart. Resume with the authenticated nextjs preview path, not the unauthenticated shell: warm tracker context online, switch offline, reload, and compare the resulting navigation/controller/cache state against the successful unauthenticated root-shell case already noted here.
2026-03-22 shared-browser authenticated reproduction after restart: using the warmed non-isolated browser context, authenticated tracker offline startup succeeded on the nextjs preview. With tracker data already loaded online, switching DevTools to Offline and reloading preserved the tracker picker and showed 'Using cached data'. Opening a fresh new tab to the same preview while still offline in that warmed shared context also succeeded. The remaining discrepancy is therefore context-specific rather than a blanket authenticated-startup failure; compare any failing browser against this passing shared-context path (service-worker version/install state, cache presence, and whether the browser context was already warmed/authenticated).
2026-03-22 additional no-cache-path check in a clean isolated browser: cleared IndexedDB, caches, and the active service worker, reloaded online once to let the route shell reinstall its worker/cache, then switched Offline and reloaded. Result: the app still served the offline sign-in shell cleanly instead of falling through to chrome-error. This confirms the basic shell failure branch is not reproducing on the current preview in either clean or warmed contexts. The remaining unverified portion of pt-780.1 is the signed-in/no-usable-tracker-cache branch (explicit failed-load messaging when tracker bootstrap data is absent), which requires preserving auth while clearing only tracker data stores.
2026-03-22 investigation update: reproduced the signed-in no-usable-IDB-cache branch setup by preserving auth_state and users while deleting ui_state.tracker_bootstrap plus activity_logs/exercises/programs in the browser. Result: normal offline reload and even a fresh offline tab still restored the tracker successfully instead of showing the explicit failed-load state. Cache Storage only contained shell assets plus Supabase auth/user, not tracker API payloads, so the repopulation appears to be coming from another warmed browser cache layer for /api/exercises, /api/programs, and /api/logs rather than IndexedDB. A hard reload / ignore-cache path can fall through to chrome-error, but that is not the normal user path. Practical takeaway: warmed authenticated offline startup is working; the remaining unresolved branch is specifically the browser-cache-purged no-usable-data case, which may need a separate acceptance decision rather than a blind code change.
title: Verify offline startup and cache fallback parity

description: Offline capability is a core product requirement because the PT office may have little or no cell service. Current Next.js offline behavior is not reliable: the tracker offline queue is still stored in localStorage, the root service worker is still legacy-static oriented, API/data bootstrap is network-only, and offline navigation falls back to /index.html instead of the current Next.js routes. This task should define and implement the migration to a unified root service worker plus IndexedDB-backed offline persistence for the Next.js app while preserving the existing static app during migration. Required scope: (1) replace localStorage queue persistence with IndexedDB for the tracker/offline write path, (2) define route-aware offline support for /, /program, /pt-view, and /rehab, (3) redesign the root service worker so it supports both legacy static surfaces and current Next.js routes during migration, (4) move service-worker registration to a shared Next.js entry point, and (5) document the architecture and rollout constraints. Constraints: do not introduce new localStorage offline persistence; preserve the existing static app while migration is in progress; avoid overlapping Claude's toast work unless coordination is needed. Validation checklist: [ ] offline-capable app data and pending writes no longer rely on localStorage, [ ] root service worker supports current Next.js route shells without breaking legacy static pages, [ ] offline navigation no longer falls back blindly to /index.html for Next.js routes, [ ] route/bootstrap caching strategy is documented for /, /program, /pt-view, and /rehab, [ ] README and tracking docs reflect the new offline architecture.
notes: 2026-03-20 session handoff: landed and pushed IndexedDB offline/bootstrap work for /pt-view, /program, and the tracker queue/auth foundation (commit 34fd98b after 7c5384a). Closed child route-bootstrap beads pt-780.10.1 and pt-780.10.2. Beads cleanup also closed pt-ant, pt-t9s, pt-bdf.1, and pt-bdf; downgraded pt-477.1, pt-1qq.1, pt-1qq.2, and pt-zdk; and added missing related links from pt-41a and pt-zb3 to this offline-architecture parent. Next implementation focus is pt-780.10.3: add IndexedDB-backed offline mutation queue for /program editor writes (exercise CRUD, roles, dosage, vocab) because offline editing in the PT office is a must-have. Keep pt-41a and pt-zb3 open as active index offline-read/cold-open work; they were not completed by the route bootstrap changes landed today.
title: Replace localStorage offline persistence and legacy SW routing with IndexedDB-backed Next.js offline architecture

This task should add IndexedDB-backed cached bootstrap data for /pt-view, wire the page to read cached data when offline and refresh when online, and update the root service worker so offline navigation to /pt-view does not fall back blindly to /index.html. close_reason: Fixed in commit 42790c3. ExerciseForm.handleSave now validates pt_category and pattern before setSaving(), blocking submission (and queuing) when either is empty.
description:
The ExerciseForm allows 'Create Exercise' with pt_category left at the 'Select...' placeholder (empty string). The form queues the mutation offline and shows an optimistic success state, but the API requires a non-empty pt_category and returns 400 on replay. The mutation is then stuck as 'failed' with no path to retry or correct it.

Root cause: pt_category select has value='' as a valid unselected state but no client-side required validation before the form submits to the queue.

Fix: add validation before queueing the exercise.create mutation — if pt_category is empty, show an inline error and block submission. Same check should apply to any other API-required fields (pattern may also be required by the API).

File scope: ExerciseForm.js (or whichever component owns the create form submit handler)
title: ExerciseForm: validate required fields before offline queue

description:

Status: open

Issue:
Verify that session save uses queue-first semantics: queue push before network attempt, sync badge updates immediately, and local history is prepended before network confirms.

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-offline-and-timing-parity/spec.md
The spec mandates this exact save order: trim notes → apply backdate → push to offline queue → update sync badge immediately → attempt immediate network sync → prepend session into local history → clear active tracker state → return to picker → re-render adherence → show "Saved (with notes)" or "Saved (no notes)". Network success is not the first visible save event — the queue push and UI update must happen first. The user is returned to picker and sees save feedback before sync outcome is known.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code
- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec
- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass
- Do not introduce new behavior not described in the spec

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Session is pushed into the offline queue before any network sync is attempted
- [ ] Sync badge is updated immediately after queue push (not after network response)
- [ ] Session is prepended into local history immediately after queue push (not after network response)
- [ ] Active tracker state (currentSession, currentExercise) is cleared after local prepend
- [ ] User is returned to picker and sees "Saved (with notes)" or "Saved (no notes)" before sync outcome
- [ ] Adherence is re-rendered immediately after returning to picker

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: n/a
- blocks: n/a

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a
notes:
2026-03-19 Verification pass: PARTIAL FAIL — items 3, 5, 6 fail. Root cause: handleSaveFinishedSession (useTrackerSession.js lines 119-140) awaits sync before doing UX updates. Item 3 FAIL: setOptimisticLogs (line 130) is AFTER await sync (line 129) — prepend should be immediate. Item 5 FAIL: showSaveSuccess (line 134) is after await sync; also handleSaveAndShowHistory goes to history tab not picker tab. Item 6 FAIL: follows from item 5 — user goes to history, picker adherence not immediately visible. Items 1, 2, 4 PASS. Fix needed: fire-and-forget sync, do UX updates (optimisticLogs prepend, modal close, state clear, feedback) immediately after enqueue, handle sync result async. Tab destination (history vs picker) also needs review against spec.
2026-03-19 Re-verify after pt-780.9 fix: ALL 6 CHECKLIST ITEMS NOW PASS. Item 3: setOptimisticLogs now called immediately after enqueue, before sync. Item 5: showSaveSuccess now called immediately before sync; tab goes to exercises (picker). Item 6: adherence re-renders on picker immediately. Closing.
title: Verify queue-first save and sync badge parity

description:
Source Reference: nextjs-parity-governance
Status: open
Priority: P2

File Scope:

Issue:
Verify that reconnect updates connectivity/sync UI immediately, attempts queue sync when context is available, performs picker-only full refresh, and does not interrupt active logger or history views.

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-offline-and-timing-parity/spec.md
When connectivity returns: update connectivity state and sync UI immediately (even if the user is not on the picker). Attempt queue sync when authenticated state and patient context (viewingPatientId) exist. Full tracker data refresh happens only when the picker is active — if the user is in logger or history view, suppress the full picker refresh and instead hydrate cache or defer. After successful sync: reload history and hydrate cache. Immediate status feedback must be preserved even when the full refresh is suppressed.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code

Issue:
Verify that offline queue uses localStorage key pt_offline_queue, mutation field client_mutation_id tied to session id, and treats 409 responses as successful queue drain.

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-offline-and-timing-parity/spec.md
The offline queue is stored under localStorage key pt_offline_queue (user-scoped: pt_offline_queue_${userId}). Each queued session includes the field client_mutation_id set to a stable identity derived from the session id. When syncOfflineQueue() posts a queued session and receives a 409 duplicate response, the session must be removed from the queue and treated as sync success — not left stuck. Queue is cleared item by item on sync success or duplicate-safe handling.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code
- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec
- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass
- Do not introduce new behavior not described in the spec

- [ ] Each queued session payload includes the field client_mutation_id
- [ ] client_mutation_id is derived from the session id (stable identity)
- [ ] A 409 response during sync causes the queued session to be removed from the queue (treated as success)
- [ ] Queue is cleared item by item (not all at once) as each session syncs successfully
- [ ] Queue is cleared on sign-out (user-scoped key ensures correct isolation)

(2) buildApiPayload passes client_mutation_id through (line 72). (3) client_mutation_id = finalSession.sessionId (useTrackerSession.js line 125). (4) res.ok || res.status === 409 → removeFromQueue (useIndexOfflineQueue.js line 86). (5) sync loops over sessions individually with for...of (line 74). (6) handleSignOut calls clearQueue() before signOut (index.js line 188). Closing.
title: Verify duplicate-safe sync parity

Issue:
Verify that offline queue migration normalizes old-format records and collapses hold-bug sessions using the exact static bug-signature collapse rule.

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-offline-and-timing-parity/spec.md
When the stored offline queue contains older session formats, the tracker must normalize them. The hold-bug collapse rule: multiple old sets with both reps AND seconds null must be collapsed into one hold set. This exact signature (reps null AND seconds null on multiple sets in the same session) is the trigger for collapse — not a generic "null set" condition. This migration must happen when the queue is loaded, before any sync attempt.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code
- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec
- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass
- Do not introduce new behavior not described in the spec

- [ ] Queue migration runs when the stored queue is loaded, before any sync attempt
- [ ] Old-format queue entries are normalized to the current session shape
- [ ] Multiple sets with both reps: null AND seconds: null in the same session are collapsed into one hold set
- [ ] The collapse rule requires both reps AND seconds to be null (not just one field null)
- [ ] Normal sets (with reps or seconds values) are not affected by the collapse rule
- [ ] Migration handles the case where the queue is empty or absent gracefully

Polling: first message check immediately after bootstrap, then every 30 seconds while signed in.

close_reason: Fix already implemented: fire-and-forget sync in useTrackerSession.js (lines 138-143), UX updates happen immediately after enqueue, and handleSaveAndShowHistory returns to picker (exercises tab). Verified in code — no changes needed.
description:
handleSaveFinishedSession in hooks/useTrackerSession.js currently awaits sync before doing any UX updates. This violates queue-first semantics: the user sees nothing until the network responds. Fix: after enqueue(payload), fire sync([payload]) without await, then immediately do all UX updates (setOptimisticLogs prepend, handleNotesModalClose, abandonDraftSession, setActiveExercise null, showSaveSuccess). Handle sync result asynchronously (e.g. via .then()). Also review tab destination: spec says 'return to picker' but handleSaveAndShowHistory goes to history tab — confirm correct behavior with spec owner.

NOTE: May overlap with Codex sync fix in progress (user flagged an active sync fix). Coordinate before implementing.
notes: Implemented on 2026-03-19. handleSaveFinishedSession now enqueues first, applies optimistic/local UX updates immediately, clears the active exercise, returns control to the picker path, and runs sync([payload]) as fire-and-forget async work. pages/index.js now keeps the user on the exercises tab after final save. Build passes.
title: Fix queue-first save flow: fire-and-forget sync, immediate UX updates

Implemented fix now uses `activity_type: inferActivityType(exercise)` in catch fallback and normalizes queued sets with inferred type. This is a DN-048 sub-fix and closes with DN-048 after DN-058 verification passes.

Constraints/Caveats:
Fix: change `activity_type,` to `activity_type: inferActivityType(exercise),` in the catch block queuedPayload. Do not move `const activityType` before the try block — that would expose it as mutable. Just call inferActivityType again in the catch block. Verification is tracked in DN-058.
title: [DN-057] ReferenceError in catch block offline-fallback path: `activity_type` shorthand (line ~215) references an undeclared variable. `activityType` (camelCase) is declared with `const` inside the `try` block and is out of scope in the `catch` block.

close_reason: Policy defined: unassigned+agent:shared for either-agent work; explicit claim before starting.
description: Source Reference: child of pt-am6 created from tracker hygiene discussion on 2026-03-17. Produce an independent Claude proposal for how Beads assignments should work when agents discover new issues. Problem: the discovering agent often tags the issue to itself, which means the other agent may never even see it while looking for work. Review the current workflow and propose concrete rules for: when discovered issues should stay assigned to the finder, when they should be left unassigned, when they should move into a shared pool, and whether a third shared/unassigned category should be used for work either Codex or Claude can do. Focus on practical rules that improve visibility without creating chaos.
notes: Policy decided: (1) Discovered issues should NOT be auto-assigned to the finder. (2) If work clearly fits one agent → assign + label agent:codex or agent:claude. (3) If either agent can do it → leave unassigned + label agent:shared. This surfaces in bd ready for both agents. (4) Claiming is explicit: bd update <id> --claim when an agent starts the work. (5) The agent:shared label already exists in BEADS_TEMPLATE.md — no new infrastructure needed.
title: Claude pass: revise assignment rules for discovered work

Issue:
On the phone screen, fields overlap inside the index log modal.
Context:
User reported that the logger modal on the index route has overlapping fields on a phone-sized screen.
Constraints/Caveats:
- Treat this as a mobile layout bug
- Preserve existing form fields and logging behavior while fixing layout
## Acceptance Criteria
- Overlapping fields in the index log modal are identified and resolved on phone-sized screens
- The modal remains usable without fields covering each other

Validation Checklist:

Dependencies:

- blocks: n/a
Reactivation Trigger:
Reopen if the overlap persists on phone screens after the layout fix.
notes:
Review-only context added 2026-03-23: Checked the nextjs preview in a 430x932 mobile viewport (iPhone 14 Pro Max-style sizing). I could open the Log Set modal on index, but did not reproduce field overlap on the Long Arc Quad exercise in that specific runtime pass. The most likely code surface is SessionLoggerModal layout: pt-rebuild/components/SessionLoggerModal.module.css:101 uses repeat(auto-fit, minmax(180px, 1fr)) for the main field grid, :107 uses repeat(auto-fit, minmax(220px, 1fr)) for formDataGrid, and :113 fixes withUnit to 1fr 90px. On narrower modal content or exercises with more required fields/units, those minimums could force cramped wrapping or overlap. Relevant runtime close surface is pt-rebuild/components/SessionLoggerModal.js:97. Suggested repro: use a phone-width viewport on index, open an exercise with more required form parameters than Long Arc Quad, then inspect Set cards in the Log Set modal for wrap/overlap issues.
Additional user-provided evidence 2026-03-23: screenshot shows the index Log Set modal on phone with the Set 1 card crowded/clipped around the Reps / Side row and Weight row, with the right-side unit control and edge spacing leaving the field area visibly compressed. Treat this as confirmed mobile layout evidence even though my own runtime pass on Long Arc Quad did not fully reproduce the same clipping state. Relevant layout surfaces remain pt-rebuild/components/SessionLoggerModal.module.css:101, :107, :113, and :155.
Additional user-provided evidence 2026-03-23: screenshot shows the index Log Set modal on phone with the Set 1 card crowded/clipped around the Reps / Side row and Weight row, with the right-side unit control and edge spacing leaving the field area visibly compressed. Treat this as confirmed mobile layout evidence even though my own runtime pass on Long Arc Quad did not fully reproduce the same clipping state. Relevant layout surfaces remain pt-rebuild/components/SessionLoggerModal.module.css:101, :107, :113, and :155.
User clarification after runtime mobile review: the layout problem is not exercise-specific and is not caused by Weight because the trailing unit selector stays very narrow ('lb'). The general failure mode is any paired row where both columns need meaningful width, such as Reps + Side and similar two-column field combinations. In the current iPhone 14 Pro Max emulation (430x932 @ DPR 3), the Log Set modal shows that wider two-column rows are the ones that crowd/clip while Weight + unit still fits. Treat this as a general two-column mobile grid defect in SessionLoggerModal rather than a single-exercise case. Relevant code: pt-rebuild/components/SessionLoggerModal.module.css:101, 107, 113, 155-159.
Additional runtime confirmation from isolated iPhone 14 Pro Max emulation using test-patient2@pttracker.app: the issue reproduces beyond Long Arc Quad. Example: Star Taps (Right Stance) -> Log Set shows the same Reps + Side paired-row problem. DOM measurement confirms the root cause is real overflow, not just perceived crowding: SessionLoggerModal_fieldGrid computes to two 173.6px columns, but the Reps input renders at 202.4px wide, so it extends into the neighboring Side column. Weight + unit continues to fit only because the trailing unit selector stays much narrower. This supports treating the bug as a general mobile two-column field-grid overflow affecting paired inputs across exercises. Relevant code: pt-rebuild/components/SessionLoggerModal.module.css:101, 107, 113, 155-159.
title: Index log modal fields overlap on phone screens

description:
Source Reference: user lunch-time intake 2026-03-23
Status: open
Priority: P1
Risk: high
Scope: current
Agent Owner: shared
Agent Eligible: codex,claude
Tags: nextjs,index,ios,mobile,performance,battery
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/components
- pt-rebuild/hooks
- pt-rebuild/public/sw.js
Issue:
Investigate heavy battery drain during tracker use on iPhone 14 Pro Max.
Context:
User reported about 19 percent battery drain over 43 minutes on iPhone 14 Pro Max. This needs investigation for polling, rendering, timers, audio, service worker, or other high-usage causes.
Constraints/Caveats:
- Preserve required tracker behavior while investigating energy usage
- Treat the user-reported measurement as the starting symptom, not a confirmed root cause
## Acceptance Criteria
- Plausible battery-drain contributors are identified
- At least one concrete investigation or mitigation path is documented
- User-reported device context is preserved
Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Plausible battery-drain contributors are identified
- [ ] At least one concrete investigation or mitigation path is documented
- [ ] User-reported device context is preserved
Dependencies:
- parent-child: pt-9c5g
- blocks: n/a
Reactivation Trigger:
Reopen if additional iPhone battery-drain evidence changes the investigation scope.
notes:
Review-only context added 2026-03-23: No runtime power profiling was performed, but the current tracker code has several plausible battery contributors on iPhone. Index uses window.requestAnimationFrame in pt-rebuild/pages/index.js:214 during exercise selection flow. Messages poll every 30 seconds via useMessages (pt-rebuild/hooks/useMessages.js:21 and :46). Active timer mode runs a 1-second interval in pt-rebuild/hooks/useExerciseTimer.js:75. Audio/speech side effects create/resume an AudioContext at pt-rebuild/hooks/useTimerAudio.js:12-15 and queue speech/beep work at :47-59. This does not prove root cause, but it narrows the first investigation targets to polling, timer intervals, speech/audio, and any repeated rerender activity while the tracker stays open on phone. User-reported symptom preserved: about 19 percent battery drain over 43 minutes on iPhone 14 Pro Max.
MEASURED DATA (2026-03-24): 19% battery drain over 43 minutes on iPhone 14 Pro Max. 24% drain over 60 minutes. Vercel shows 0% cache hit rate — all requests hitting origin. These are the only confirmed data points. Prior notes in this bead are code-read speculation only — no runtime profiling has been performed.
MEASURED DATA (not speculation): Battery drain is consistent ~0.4%/min — 19% in 43 min and 24% in 60 min on iPhone 14 Pro Max. Consistent rate rules out one-time load spike; points to something running continuously. PRIMARY SUSPECT: /api/logs had 891 requests in 6 hours on Preview (~1 request every 24 seconds). All other endpoints were 10x lower (/api/users 92, /api/exercises 40, /api/programs 46). This is almost certainly a polling loop. ADDITIONAL DATA: INP 800ms on mobile (red/poor in Speed Insights) — main thread blocking on interaction. Vercel shows 3.9K Supabase API calls in 6 hours from preview (small user count). Data Cache and Runtime Cache both 0 — expected, app uses client-side Supabase JS, bypasses Next.js data cache entirely. Speed Insights shows no mobile data — likely PWA standalone mode not firing beacon. EXISTING NOTES ON BEAD ARE SPECULATION from code read, not profiling data. NEXT STEP: Find what is calling /api/logs on ~24s interval.
MEASURED DATA (not speculation):
- Battery drain: ~0.4%/min sustained — 19% in 43min and 24% in 60min on iPhone 14 Pro Max. Consistent rate points to continuous activity, not a one-time load spike.
- Vercel Fast Data Transfer (Preview, 6hrs): /api/logs had 891 requests — 10x more than any other endpoint (/api/users 92, /api/exercises 40, /api/programs 46). One request every ~24 seconds = likely a polling loop.
- Supabase: ~3.9K API calls in 6 hours from preview (small user count).
- Vercel Speed Insights (Mobile): INP 800ms (red/poor). Main thread is blocked 800ms on interactions. Combined with polling = CPU pegged continuously.
- Vercel Data Cache: 0 reads/0 writes — expected, app uses Supabase client-side not Next.js server fetch.
- Speed Insights not reporting on Mobile/PWA: standalone mode beacon may not fire. Desktop reports fine. Data untrustworthy for mobile until resolved.
PRIMARY SUSPECT: something polling /api/logs every ~24 seconds. Find and fix this first before investigating other contributors. Notes above this line are MEASURED. Notes below (if any from prior sessions) are SPECULATION from code read only.
title: Investigate heavy iPhone battery drain during tracker use

Issue:
Match static hold-timer completion behavior exactly. Every timed rep completion plays the triple completion beep, advances rep state, and then speaks milestone phrases based on repsLeft after the timed rep completes.

Static Source Anchors:
- initTimerMode(): public/index.html around lines 2692-2718
- startTimer(): public/index.html around lines 2775-2803
- playCompletionSound(): public/index.html around lines 2894-2903

Static Rules:
- Hold mode uses currentExercise.seconds_per_rep and currentExercise.current_reps
- When remaining hits zero, play completion triple beep
- Then compute repsLeft = totalReps - currentRep
- Speak Set complete when repsLeft <= 0
- Speak Last rep when repsLeft === 1
- Speak 3 reps left when repsLeft === 3
- Speak 5 reps left when repsLeft === 5
- Advance currentRep and reset display to full target duration for the next rep

Constraints/Caveats:
- Preserve the static moment at which Last rep is spoken, even if it differs from counter mode interaction timing
- Do not close until linked parity beads ptrebuild-spg.3 and ptrebuild-spg.10 are verified or closed

description:
Source Reference: architecture decision approved on 2026-03-17 after static cue trace and migration review
Status: in_progress
Priority: P1
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: nextjs,migration,architecture,logging,timer,audio,speech,state-machine
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/lib/logger-timer-machine.js (new)
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useExerciseTimer.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useTimerSpeech.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useTimerAudio.js
- pt-rebuild/components/TimerPanel.js
- pt-rebuild/components/PocketModeOverlay.js

Issue:
Build the long-term expandable logger timer/cue core for Next.js in the final architecture shape now, not as a temporary helper. The goal is to stop rebuilding timer/audio/speech behavior by moving logger execution into an explicit state/event/transition core with emitted cues/effects.

Architecture Decision:
- This is NOT a patch layer and NOT disposable scaffolding
- This must be fully capable and pre-structured to expand into a fuller logger execution domain later without replacement
- Future growth must extend these blocks, not throw them away
- A full generic event bus is not required now, but the timer/cue domain must already use state-machine-style boundaries

Required Shape:
1. Domain core in lib/logger-timer-machine.js
   - pure transition logic
   - explicit state object
   - explicit event/intention vocabulary
   - transition result includes next state plus emitted cue/effect intents
2. Adapter hooks
   - useExerciseTimer.js becomes a thin adapter around the domain core for timer state progression
   - useTimerSpeech.js / useTimerAudio.js execute emitted effects (beeps, speech, queue clear, audio unlock) rather than owning business rules
3. Thin UI
   - TimerPanel and PocketModeOverlay render state and dispatch intents
   - UI should not own cue sequencing rules

Minimum Event Vocabulary:
- OPEN_EXERCISE
- CLOSE_EXERCISE
- SELECT_SIDE
- INCREMENT_COUNTER
- DECREMENT_COUNTER
- START_TIMER
- PAUSE_TIMER
- RESET_TIMER
- TIMER_TICK
- TIMER_ZERO_REACHED
- POCKET_OPEN
- POCKET_CLOSE
- POCKET_LONG_PRESS
- APPLY_SET

Minimum State Coverage:
- mode (reps, hold, duration, distance)
- selected side
- counter value
- elapsed / remaining time
- current rep / total reps
- completed reps
- canApply / set-ready state
- pocket-open state
- any cue-relevant fields needed to avoid duplicate countdown or stale speech behavior

Minimum Effect/Cue Output Types:
- play_soft_tick
- play_countdown_warning
- play_completion_triple
- play_partial_confirm
- speak_text
- clear_speech_queue
- ensure_audio_ready

Mode Policy Requirement:
- Shared mechanism, explicit per-mode rules
- Do NOT flatten static differences across counter, hold, duration, and Pocket Mode
- The machine must be able to express:
  - counter tap beep + repsLeft milestone speech
  - hold rep-end completion beep + repsLeft milestone speech
  - duration timer-end completion beep + Set complete only
  - Start/Pause speech threshold > 10 seconds
  - Pocket long-press partial cue

Non-Negotiable Constraint:
This implementation must be expansion-safe. If later we need a fuller logger execution engine, analytics, richer Pocket behavior, or broader event routing, this core must be incorporated directly. No replacement architecture.

Migration Plan:
- Step 1: codify current timer/counter domain state and events in the new machine file
- Step 2: move cue decision rules out of hooks/components and into machine transitions / effect outputs
- Step 3: refactor existing hooks into adapters around the machine
- Step 4: keep UI components thin and driven by machine state
- Step 5: validate against the child parity beads under ptrebuild-9v1

Closure Gate:
- Do not close this implementation bead until linked child parity beads under ptrebuild-9v1 that it is meant to satisfy are verified or closed
- This bead is the implementation core; the ptrebuild-9v1 subtree remains the acceptance surface

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] New machine file exists and owns timer/counter transition logic
- [ ] Hooks are adapters, not primary owners of cue business rules
- [ ] TimerPanel / PocketModeOverlay dispatch intents and render machine state rather than encoding cue rules inline
- [ ] Static cue differences across counter, hold, duration, and Pocket Mode can be expressed without special-case sprawl across multiple files
- [ ] Future expansion would extend this architecture rather than replace it
notes:
2026-03-17 behavior decisions from user before implementation:\n1. Counter milestone timing: keep current 5/3/1 milestone semantics with counter starting at 0. Preserve current behavior rather than redefining Last rep semantics. Key concern is delayed speech; implementation should make milestone cue timing reliable at 5/3/1.\n2. Hold completion cues: preserve static-style hold rep completion behavior, including rep-end completion cue behavior and milestone speech at 5/3/1.\n3. Duration completion speech: do NOT keep plain static Set complete only. Preferred richer speech is set X of Y complete for [right|left] side when side context exists.\n4. Start/pause policy: add an explicit start confirmation beep for timers >= 5 seconds so the user knows the press registered. Pause should be spoken when target > 5 seconds OR when in Pocket Mode.\nOpen clarification still needed before finalizing comparison-speech policy: how to choose the comparison baseline for phrases like X more reps than last time / X fewer reps than last time when prior sessions contain multiple sets with different counts.
2026-03-17 comparison-speech decision: do not wire live comparison speech in the first machine implementation. Keep the architecture capable of adding it later, but leave it disabled/unhooked for now. Rationale: simple previous-set comparison creates misleading plus/minus swings across multi-set progressions (example progression: 15,15 -> 20,10 -> 25,5 is overall progress but naive per-set comparison reads as mixed improvement/regression). Preserve an extension point or clear code comment for future implementation rather than shipping misleading comparison speech now.
2026-03-17 future comparison-speech direction from user: if comparison speech is added later, prefer progress signals that do not punish multi-set redistribution. Candidate rules: (1) announce improvement when reps in any current set exceed the largest rep count from any comparable set in the last instance; (2) announce improvement/regression using total reps across all sets for the relevant worked side/context rather than naive immediate previous-set comparison. For sided exercises, totals should respect the side actually worked so right-only or left-only progress is not distorted.
2026-03-17 refined future comparison-speech model: for the relevant exercise and side context, compare current session against the comparable prior session using two metrics: (1) prior_best_set / current_best_set = max reps in any comparable set; (2) prior_total_reps / current_total_reps = sum of reps across all comparable sets. Suggested future speech priority: first, if current_best_set > prior_best_set, announce positive best-set improvement (for example, X more reps than your best set last time); second, if current_total_reps < prior_total_reps, announce negative total-volume regression (for example, Y fewer total reps than last time); third, optionally if current_total_reps > prior_total_reps and no stronger best-set cue fired, announce positive total-volume improvement. For sided exercises, compute and compare per side so right-only or left-only work is not mixed together.
2026-03-17 implementation progress: machine core is now active in code. Counter increment/decrement, side-change speech, timer start/pause/reset/tick/completion, and pocket long-press partial cue decisions now route through lib/logger-timer-machine.js, with hooks acting as adapters and useTimerAudio executing named effect intents. Next implementation step is to align machine behavior with newer product decisions from this session (start-confirmation beep >=5s, pause speech >5s or Pocket Mode, richer duration completion speech when the machine has enough session context).
2026-03-17 implementation progress: start/pause/reset/tick/partial timer paths are now routed through lib/logger-timer-machine.js via useExerciseTimer adapter. Pocket Mode now syncs into the machine state so pause behavior can depend on overlay context. useTimerAudio executes named machine effects, including the new start-confirmation beep effect. useTimerSpeech now passes sessionProgress into the adapter layer so duration completion speech can be upgraded from plain Set complete to set X of Y complete (including side when relevant). next build passes after these changes.
2026-03-17 milestone: machine behavior now reflects the newer session decisions instead of the older static defaults in key places. Start no longer speaks; it issues a confirmation beep for timers >= 5s. Pause speech now follows the updated rule (>5s or Pocket Mode). Pocket Mode open/close state is fed into the machine through TimerPanel so overlay context can influence cue behavior. Duration completion speech is now shaped in the adapter layer from sessionProgress context rather than hard-coded to plain Set complete.
2026-03-18 machine smoke check via direct Node import of lib/logger-timer-machine.js: counter mode emitted play_soft_tick on every increment, with speech at tap 5 (5 reps left), tap 7 (3 reps left), tap 9 (Last rep), and completion triple-beep + Set complete at tap 10 for a 10-rep target. Hold mode emitted ensure_audio_ready + play_start_confirm on start for a 10-second hold timer, countdown warnings at remaining 3/2/1, and completion triple-beep at zero for the first timed rep of a 3-rep hold set (no milestone speech yet because repsLeft became 2). Duration mode emitted ensure_audio_ready + play_start_confirm on start for a 12-second timer, countdown warning at remaining 3, and completion triple-beep + raw Set complete at zero. Note: duration completion wording is then upgraded in the adapter layer from raw Set complete to the richer set X of Y complete form when sessionProgress context is available.
2026-03-18 implementation progress: wired delayed comparison speech into the Next Set confirm flow using the approved future model. Added pure helper lib/logger-progress-comparison.js plus sessionStartedAt tracking in usePanelSessionProgress so current-session saves do not compare against logs created after the panel opened. Index page now schedules delayed speech after successful submitSeedSet with side-aware session metrics and prior-session cutoff.
2026-03-18 restart handoff: structured timer/cue machine is active in code. Current files in play: pt-rebuild/lib/logger-timer-machine.js, pt-rebuild/hooks/useExerciseTimer.js, pt-rebuild/hooks/useTimerSpeech.js, pt-rebuild/hooks/useTimerAudio.js, pt-rebuild/components/TimerPanel.js. Start uses confirm beep for timers >=5s and does not speak Start. Pause speech follows the product rule (>5s or Pocket Mode). Duration completion speech is upgraded from raw Set complete to set X of Y complete, including side when relevant. Comparison speech is now wired after successful Next Set confirmation using lib/logger-progress-comparison.js plus sessionStartedAt tracking in usePanelSessionProgress so current-session saves do not compare against logs created after the panel opened. Remaining work before closure: rerun next build after the last sessionStartedAt reset change, verify runtime behavior against the pt-9v1 child beads, and commit the refactor.
title: Build structured logger timer and cue state machine core

description:
Cutover requirement captured from 2026-03-21 discussion.

Requirement:
The nextjs branch must remain deployable on Vercel after legacy static app code is pruned out, so it can eventually replace main cleanly instead of permanently carrying both architectures.

Intent:
- Preserve the legacy static app on a separate branch/subdomain as fallback
- Prune legacy static app code from nextjs once that fallback is secured
- Keep only true shared browser-served assets in public/ on nextjs
- Verify nextjs still builds/deploys correctly on Vercel after legacy pruning
- Make eventual branch replacement (nextjs becoming the primary branch) operationally straightforward

Why this matters:
Keeping old static pages inside nextjs forever preserves unnecessary complexity and increases the chance that future agents edit the wrong surface. The fallback should live on its own branch/deployment, not inside the modern app branch.

Next-session use:
Treat this as a cutover-plan requirement, not an immediate cleanup task. Do not prune legacy code from nextjs until the fallback branch/subdomain plan is ready.

description:
Source Reference: user-confirmed iPhone bug on 2026-03-17, initially reported on `/program`
Status: open
Priority: P1
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex,claude
Tags: nextjs,ios,ui,editor,dropdown,bug
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/program.js
- pt-rebuild/pages/pt-view.js
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/components/ExerciseForm.js
- pt-rebuild/components/ExerciseFormCore.js
- pt-rebuild/components/ExerciseFormCues.js
- pt-rebuild/components/ExerciseFormLifecycle.js
- pt-rebuild/components/ExercisePicker.js
- pt-rebuild/components/SessionLoggerModal.js
- pt-rebuild/styles/globals.css

Issue:
Dropdown/select behavior across the Next.js app must be consolidated into a shared reusable UI implementation, not re-created page by page. The immediate confirmed bug is on `/program`, but the correct fix is to extract a shared iPhone-safe select component/module and then adopt it on each page that uses dropdowns.

Context:
User confirmed the bug on a real iPhone. Code review confirms the Next.js app currently uses multiple native `<select>` and datalist/select patterns across `/program`, `/index`, and `/pt-view`. `/index` already contains the most proven dropdown behavior because its form-parameter flow supports both standard native selects and an `Other...` path that reveals a text input. That working behavior should become the source for a shared reusable implementation rather than being copied manually into each page.

Required Architecture Direction:
- Implement a shared reusable select UI module/component in `components/` rather than repeating select markup/CSS/behavior per page.
- The shared implementation must support both:
  - plain native select fields
  - native select fields with an optional `Other...` mode that can be enabled or disabled per usage
- `Other...` must be configurable, not always on. Call sites need a clear prop/option to allow or disallow the fallback text-entry path.
- Keep the implementation native-select-first for iPhone/Safari compatibility.
- Do not treat this as a pure `lib/` extraction only; the reuse must cover the JSX/CSS/interaction layer, not just helper functions.

Observed Implementation Notes:
- `/program` uses native selects in the page-level selector and throughout the exercise editor form.

description:
public/reset-password.html is the current Supabase auth redirect target for password reset emails. It uses the vendored supabase.min.js client to process the recovery token and set a new password.

This should be a proper Next.js page at pages/reset-password.js, using the shared Supabase client (lib/supabase.js) and consistent app styling.

Steps:
1. Create pages/reset-password.js — handles Supabase recovery token from URL hash, shows new password form, calls supabase.auth.updateUser()
2. Update Supabase auth redirect URL in Supabase dashboard (Authentication > URL Configuration > Redirect URLs) to point to Next.js route
3. Verify password reset flow works end-to-end on preview URL before pruning the static file
4. Once verified: prune public/reset-password.html and public/js/vendor/supabase.min.js (the only remaining consumer)

Context:
- App has 2 users — this flow is rarely used but is real and must work
- Current static page is functional; do not break it before the Next.js replacement is verified
- supabase.min.js can only be pruned after this migration is complete (it has no other consumers)

- public/reset-password.html (prune after verified)

- Supabase dashboard: auth redirect URL update required
