description:
Source Reference: static index parity exploration (2026-03-15)
Status: open
Priority: P1
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex,claude
Tags: migration,ui,index,logging-flow,notes,backdate,parity
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/public/index.html
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/components/SessionLoggerModal.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useSessionLogging.js

Issue:
Next.js index lacks the static tracker finish-session flow that collects session notes and optional backdate before saving the session.

Context:
Static parity baseline lives in public/index.html. The legacy tracker keeps a route-level currentSession, blocks finish when no sets are logged, opens notesModal from finishSession(), allows optional backdate defaulted to session start time via toggleBackdate(), applies the chosen backdate to the session and each set in saveSession(), enqueues the whole session for offline sync, updates local history immediately, then returns to the picker view. Current Next.js index route renders SessionLoggerModal, TimerPanel, and NextSetConfirmModal, but the route shell does not show an equivalent tracker-level finish-session notes/backdate step, and its current logging path appears to submit through the modal directly rather than finalizing a tracker-owned session object.

Constraints/Caveats:
Reference behavior is public/index.html, especially finishSession(), toggleBackdate(), and saveSession(). Preserve existing offline queue safeguards and do not simplify away the user-facing finish step without explicit approval. Keep any eventual implementation aligned with tracker UX flow, not just data persistence.

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Next.js tracker blocks finish/save parity when no sets are logged
- [ ] Next.js tracker offers session notes and optional backdate before final save
- [ ] Backdate, when used, updates the saved session timestamp and associated set timestamps consistently
- [ ] Saved session returns the user to the tracker picker/history state with immediate local history/adherence refresh

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: pt-bdf

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a

Closure Gate:
- Do not close this implementation bead until the linked parity bead (pt-spg.9) is verified or closed.
- This bead covers implementation work; pt-spg.9 remains the final acceptance surface for session-finalization parity.
notes:
2026-03-18 implementation status: Added route-level draft-session finalization in pages/index.js with finish blocking, notes/backdate modal, backdate warning copy, queue-first final save, optimistic local history refresh, and tracker-owned manual set entry routing. Bead remains open because closure is gated by pt-spg.9 parity verification.
2026-03-18 structure follow-up: index.js was refactored back under the 500L page hard cap and is now 494 lines after extracting SessionNotesModal and index-tracker-session helpers. Because it is now close to the cap, any additional tracker-session wiring should extract into a hook or component instead of expanding the page further.
title: Next.js index missing finish-session notes/backdate/save flow from static tracker

acceptance_criteria:
- Archiving an exercise via lifecycle_status hides it from all exercise lists and selectors
- No code path writes to the `archived` boolean column
- The `archived` boolean sync block in handlers/exercises.js updateExercise is removed
- All display labels use lifecycle_status check
- The `archived` field is removed from ExerciseForm initial state
description:
The `archived` boolean column on the `exercises` table is redundant with `lifecycle_status`. Archiving an exercise sets `lifecycle_status = 'archived'` but the boolean is not always written consistently. All read paths (filters, display labels, sort) still check `exercise.archived`, so exercises archived via lifecycle_status alone still appear in lists.

The fix is to stop treating `archived` as the source of truth. All filtering and display logic should check `lifecycle_status === 'archived'`, and all write paths should stop setting the `archived` boolean (or only set `lifecycle_status`). The boolean column can be left in the DB for now and dropped in a follow-up migration.

**Affected files:**
- `api/roles.js:31` — `.eq('exercises.archived', false)` → `.neq('exercises.lifecycle_status', 'archived')`
- `components/ExerciseForm.js:11,57` — remove `archived` from initial state
- `components/ExerciseRolesWorkspace.js:40` — label check
- `components/ProgramDosageWorkspace.js:46` — label check
- `components/ProgramExerciseSelector.js:40` — label check
- `hooks/useProgramWorkspaceState.js:7,16` — filter logic
- `hooks/usePtViewData.js:61,82` — filter logic (also verify `p.exercises?.archived` is correct — exercises is an array)
- `lib/exercise-sort.js:88` — filter logic
- `lib/handlers/exercises.js:90,399,528,583,587,760` — read shape, insert, update, delete handlers (remove archived boolean writes; remove sync hack at 586-588)
- `lib/program-optimistic.js:19` — remove `archived` from optimistic shape
- `lib/pt-view.js:171` — filter logic

**Do not change** `db/migrate.js` — historical migration script.
title: Stop using archived boolean — migrate all exercise archive logic to lifecycle_status

Issue:
Keep design.md as a true migration design that explains how Next.js should satisfy the finalized baseline specs, while preserving baseline-oriented legacy design material in companion docs.

Context:
The artifact-role mismatch has been one of the main sources of confusion in this change. The design artifact originally held baseline exploration, but OpenSpec expects design.md to explain HOW the change should be implemented. baseline-design.md now preserves the older baseline-focused design, and design.md should stay in the migration-design role from this point forward.

Constraints/Caveats:
Design must be built on the proposal and specs, not on memory. Keep design.md focused on context, goals, decisions, risks, migration plan, and open questions. Do not re-collapse migration design and legacy baseline reconstruction into one file.

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] design.md explains HOW the migration should satisfy the parity contract rather than restating legacy behavior
- [ ] design.md references the finalized specs as the source contract and baseline companion docs as supporting evidence
- [ ] baseline-design.md remains preserved as historical baseline-oriented design instead of being lost or silently overwritten
- [ ] An agent reading proposal + specs + design can understand both the parity target and the intended migration approach

Issue:
The Next.js /program editor changed equipment, muscles, and form-parameter entry from the static select-plus-Other model to a free-text-plus-datalist TagSection pattern.

Confirmed findings:
- Static pt_editor uses select controls populated from /api/reference-data
- Static provides an explicit Other option and separate custom input path
- Next.js uses TagSection with free text input and datalist suggestions instead
This is not a hardcoded-value bug, but it is a real interaction and parity drift that can affect iPhone behavior and accidental free-form entry.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Keep reference-data suggestions available
- Preserve the ability to enter custom values when intended
- Coordinate with shared NativeSelect usage if this becomes a shared helper change

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Equipment entry path matches the intended static or approved shared behavior
- [ ] Muscle entry path matches the intended static or approved shared behavior
- [ ] Form-parameter entry path matches the intended static or approved shared behavior
- [ ] Custom entry remains available only where intended
- [ ] iPhone interaction risk is not increased by the chosen pattern

- blocks: n/a

Reopen if /program reference-data entry remains behaviorally inconsistent with the agreed shared pattern.
notes: Direction confirmed on 2026-03-19: /program should use NativeSelect with explicit Other for equipment, muscles, and form parameters. Free-text-first suggestion entry is not desired because it increases duplicate risk and requires remembering existing values.
title: Align /program reference-data entry interaction with static select-plus-Other behavior

Issue:
Split static app to legacy.pttracker.app subdomain and flip Next.js to production at pttracker.app.

Context:
Currently: main branch (static app) serves pttracker.app. nextjs branch serves preview URL only.
Hobby plan constraint: no branch-level domain routing in one Vercel project — requires a second Vercel project (pt-static) for the static app at the subdomain.
Plan: (1) Create static branch from current main. (2) Create pt-static Vercel project pointing to static branch. (3) Assign legacy.pttracker.app to pt-static. (4) When Next.js parity confirmed: merge nextjs to main, pttracker.app becomes Next.js, legacy.pttracker.app stays on pt-static/static branch.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Hobby plan: second Vercel project required for persistent static subdomain
- Do NOT redirect pttracker.app to subdomain — breaks PWA scope for installed app
- Do not flip production until pre-cutover gate bead passes
- pt-b65 (reset-password migration) must complete before flip — post-flip pttracker.app is Next.js, reset-password.html lives on legacy subdomain only

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] legacy.pttracker.app serves static app correctly
- [ ] pttracker.app serves Next.js after flip
- [ ] Supabase auth works post-flip (sign in, session, reset-password via new Next.js route)
- [ ] PWA offline behavior verified on pttracker.app post-flip
- [ ] legacy.pttracker.app still serves static (not Next.js) after flip

description:
Source Reference: pt-cf0
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: low
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: cutover,deployment,git,vercel

Issue:
Create a static branch from current main and add a temporary redirect so legacy.pttracker.app redirects to pttracker.app.

Context:
The static branch will be deployed via a second Vercel project (pt-static) to legacy.pttracker.app. While the static app is still the primary app at pttracker.app, legacy.pttracker.app should redirect there rather than serve duplicate content. The redirect is removed post-flip (pt-cf0.7).

Implementation:
1. Create static branch from main (git checkout -b static origin/main or equivalent)
2. In pt-rebuild/vercel.json on the static branch, add a redirect rule:
   { "source": "/", "destination": "https://pttracker.app", "permanent": false }
   (or use a catch-all source: "/:path*" so all paths redirect)
3. Push static branch to origin

Constraints/Caveats:
- Branch from main, not from nextjs
- Use a 302 (temporary) redirect, not 301 — will be removed post-flip
- Catch-all redirect preferred so no URL on legacy.pttracker.app serves content directly yet
- Do not make any other code changes on this branch

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] static branch exists on origin
- [ ] static branch vercel.json contains catch-all 302 redirect to pttracker.app
- [ ] static branch matches main otherwise exactly

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: pt-cf0
- blocks: n/a

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a
notes: 2026-03-22 implementation update: created shared branch static from main and pruned it to the legacy runtime surface instead of keeping a full main snapshot. Kept pt-rebuild/public, pt-rebuild/api, pt-rebuild/lib/{auth,db,handlers}, pt-rebuild/vercel.json, and a minimal pt-rebuild/package.json/package-lock.json; removed unrelated repo docs, migration/history surfaces, Next.js-only structures, and branch-local notes. Branch docs now describe static as the frozen legacy fallback surface.
title: Create static branch from current main

description:

Status: open

Issue:
Remove the temporary redirect from legacy.pttracker.app → pttracker.app so the static branch serves the static app directly.

Context:
After the production flip (pt-cf0.5), pttracker.app serves Next.js and legacy.pttracker.app should serve the static app as a real fallback — not redirect away. The 302 redirect added in pt-cf0.1 must be removed from vercel.json on the static branch at this point.

PRE-REQUISITE (before removing redirect):
Copy all environment variables from pt-rehab Vercel project to pt-static project. The static API routes need SUPABASE_URL, SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY, and any other vars present in pt-rehab. Without these, the static app will load but API calls will fail. Do this via Vercel dashboard: vercel.com/pt-tracker/pt-static/settings/environment-variables. Copy all vars from pt-rehab settings before proceeding.

Implementation:
1. Copy env vars to pt-static (manual step in Vercel dashboard)
2. Remove the redirect rule from pt-rebuild/vercel.json on the static branch
3. Push to origin — pt-static Vercel project will redeploy automatically

Constraints/Caveats:
- Do not remove redirect until pt-cf0.5 (flip) is confirmed successful
- Copy env vars BEFORE removing redirect — otherwise the static app will be broken the moment redirect is removed
- Only change in vercel.json: remove the redirect rule

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Env vars copied to pt-static project
- [ ] Redirect rule removed from vercel.json on static branch

- [ ] Sign in works on legacy.pttracker.app

description:
Source Reference: discovered during fresh-page responsive review of /pt-view on 2026-03-21
Status: in_progress
Priority: P2
Risk: low
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: nextjs,pt-view,responsive,ui,agent:codex
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/components/PtViewFiltersPanel.module.css

Issue:
The /pt-view filters panel lets the Search field keep too much width at medium window sizes, so it can visually overlap the date-range inputs before the layout reflows.

Context:
During a fresh-page Playwright review of the deployed preview, the filters panel looked cramped at medium widths and the Search input could collide with the date-range controls before wrapping to the next row. The panel needs stronger width constraints and earlier responsive reflow so the fields remain readable across smaller desktop and tablet window sizes.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Keep the existing filter fields and behavior unchanged
- Fix with scoped pt-view filter layout rules rather than page-level hacks
- Do not regress wider desktop layout or narrow mobile stacking

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Search input no longer overlaps the date-range fields at medium widths
- [ ] Filters panel still renders as a compact multi-column layout on wider desktop widths
- [ ] Filters panel stacks cleanly on narrow widths
- [ ] No functional behavior changes to filter inputs

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: pt-h5o
- blocks: n/a

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a
notes:
2026-03-21 implementation: added min-width:0 and width:100% constraints for filter controls, constrained the date-range grid to minmax(0,1fr) tracks, and added earlier 2-column then 1-column breakpoints so the Search field reflows before it can collide with the date inputs. Temporary Playwright style injection on a fresh preview page confirmed the overlap was removed at medium and narrow widths.
2026-03-21 deployed verification: pushed in commit f21b817 and rechecked /pt-view on a fresh dark-mode page at 650px wide. The filters panel now reflows before the Search field can collide with the date-range controls, while the wider multi-column layout remains intact.
title: Fix pt-view filters responsive overlap

description:

Risk: medium

Tags: nextjs,migration,ui

Issue:
DN-064 fix incorrectly opens SessionLoggerModal (form) on exercise tap; original behavior opens the full-screen TimerPanel (counter/timer), and Codex previously reverted the same UX (DN-065 revert) per user direction.

Context:
Original flow (public/index.html):
- Exercise tap → full-screen logger view (showView('logger')) = TimerPanel
  - Exercise name + dosage header
  - Side selector (Left/Right)
  - Large circular rep counter (tap to count) OR timer (for hold/duration exercises)
  - Previous / Log Set / Next Set / Done / Pocket Mode / Back to Exercises

Two logging paths within TimerPanel:
- Log Set = enter data myself (manual reps/params entry — any reason: retrospective,
  hands occupied, counter not used).
  Form modal: ALL fields default to prescribed dosage (reps, time/hold duration, form
  parameters like band_resistance, etc.) and form_data pre-filled from last used values.
  Side selector included. → Save Set
- Next Set = use app data (what counter/timer recorded) and move on.
  Confirmation modal: shows recorded data, form_data fields, side → Cancel / Edit / Log & Next

What our DN-064 fix did wrong:
- Commit 321fda1 added logger.openCreate(selected) inside handleExerciseSelect, wiring
  exercise tap directly to SessionLoggerModal (skipping TimerPanel entirely)
- Commit 9b586a9 fixed a TDZ crash from the same change
- Codex had already done the same for DN-065 and reverted per user direction (commit 59d4486)
- We re-introduced the same reverted UX via DN-064

Phase 4c (ptrebuild-weq) and 4d (ptrebuild-ryf) are both in_progress with Codex.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Do NOT wire exercise tap to SessionLoggerModal or any modal — tap opens TimerPanel
- Exercise tap in current state should only SELECT the exercise until TimerPanel is ready
- Wait for Codex 4c + 4d to complete before re-wiring
- TDZ fix (moving logger declaration above handleExerciseSelect) — KEEP regardless
- onPointerUp must remain (iOS-safe)
- SessionLoggerModal = Log Set and Next Set modals used from WITHIN TimerPanel

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] logger.openCreate() is NOT called from handleExerciseSelect
- [ ] Exercise tap selects/highlights the exercise only
- [ ] SessionLoggerModal does NOT open on exercise tap
- [ ] No TDZ crash on SSR (logger declaration stays above handleExerciseSelect)
- [ ] Exercise tap → TimerPanel; TimerPanel → Log Set / Next Set modals
- [ ] Log Set form defaults ALL fields to prescribed dosage (reps, time, hold duration, form params)
- [ ] Log Set form_data fields (band_resistance etc.) pre-filled from last used values

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: ptrebuild-bdf (DN-064)
- blocks: nothing directly; must be resolved before 4i cutover
- waiting-on: ptrebuild-weq (DN-048), ptrebuild-ryf (DN-049)

Codex closes ptrebuild-weq and ptrebuild-ryf (4c + 4d complete)

description:
## Summary
Track wording, safety prompts, delete behavior, and information architecture improvements for the /program vocabulary editor.

## Scope
This parent covers open questions about opening warnings, delete confirmations, soft-delete behavior, dependent exercise handling, wording updates, and an optional advanced disclosure area.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Deletion and dependency behavior is explicitly specified before implementation
- Copy updates are documented in user-friendly terms

Parent only; implementation should happen in child beads.
title: Program vocab editor UX and safety improvements

description:

Decide whether opening the /program vocabulary editor should show a warning or require confirmation before the editor is displayed.

- Trigger conditions and copy are documented
- The decision is traceable for later implementation

## Notes
Requested as child 2.1 under the vocab editor parent.
title: Warn or confirm when opening vocab editor

description:
## Summary
Define the expected delete behavior for pt_category entries in the /program vocabulary editor, including confirmation, soft-delete expectations, and handling for assigned exercises.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Separate child beads cover delete confirmation and dependency handling
- Delete behavior is specified clearly enough for implementation

## Notes
Requested as child 2.2 under the vocab editor parent.
title: Define vocab editor deletion behaviors

description:
## Summary
Add a delete confirmation flow for vocab editor entries and confirm that deletion is a soft delete rather than destructive removal.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Delete requires explicit confirmation
- The persistence model is verified to be soft delete or updated accordingly

## Notes
Requested as child 2.2.1.
title: Confirm vocab entry deletion and verify soft delete

description:
## Summary
Define what should happen when a pt_category is deleted while exercises are still assigned to it.

## Decision
Pending. This bead exists to capture and resolve the decision before implementation.

## Rationale
Deleting an in-use pt_category could leave active exercises in an invalid or confusing state, so the allowed behavior needs to be defined intentionally.

## Alternatives Considered
- Prohibit deletion while active exercises are assigned
- Require reassignment before delete
- Allow deletion but bulk-reassign affected exercises during the flow

## Acceptance Criteria
- The allowed delete behavior is explicitly decided
- Required safeguards or reassignment flow is documented

## Notes
Requested as child 2.2.2 under the vocab editor deletion-behavior parent.
title: Decide how deleted pt_category values affect assigned exercises

description:
## Summary
Replace internal wording like code and definition in the vocab editor with more human-friendly labels and helper descriptions, and add a Unicode warning symbol in a triangle with cautionary copy about changing values only when certain.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Existing technical labels/helper copy are identified
- Replacement wording is more understandable for non-technical users
- Warning copy and placement are specified

## Notes
Requested as child 2.3.
title: Rewrite vocab editor copy in human-friendly language

description:

Consider adding an Advanced disclosure section in the vocab editor using the same sideways/down triangle affordance used by sections like Basic information, Equipment, and Muscles.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A decision is made on whether to add an Advanced section
- If approved, the disclosure pattern should match existing editor sections

## Notes
Requested as child 2.4.
title: Consider adding an Advanced disclosure to vocab editor

File Scope:

Issue:
The Next.js tracker route is still passing session.user.id (Supabase auth UUID) into tracker bootstrap instead of the resolved users.id patient/profile context from useUserContext. That causes /api/programs?patient_id=<auth UUID> to return 403 on preview and blocks truthful offline/reconnect verification.

Context:
useUserContext already resolves both profileId and patientId. index-data fetches programs by patient_id and expects the resolved patient context, not the auth UUID. The same route already uses profileId for messages because auth UUID and users.id are different domains.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Keep queue persistence keyed by auth userId where that is the intentional user-scoped storage key
- Use resolved patient/profile context only where tracker data APIs require users.id ownership
- Do not regress therapist/patient route behavior

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] /api/programs no longer requests patient_id=session.user.id
- [ ] Tracker loads programs/exercises/logs again on preview for the signed-in test account
- [ ] Offline/reconnect verification can proceed against a real populated tracker state

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: pt-780.3
- blocks: pt-780.3.1

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a
notes: 2026-03-22 implementation update: index route now threads tracker bootstrap and session logging through userCtx.patientId instead of session.user.id so /api/programs uses the resolved users.id patient/profile context. Next step is live DevTools verification that the 403 request disappears and the tracker picker repopulates.
title: Tracker bootstrap uses auth UUID instead of resolved patient context

- [ ] Exactly two nav tabs are present and are labeled "Exercises" and "History" (not more, not fewer)
- [ ] Logger is a dedicated owned shell view separate from both tabs, not a picker overlay or a third tab
- [ ] Header remains sticky and persistent when user switches between picker, logger, and history views

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-shell-and-context-parity/spec.md
The spec requires both dates be normalized to local midnight before computing day difference, "Done today" for 0-day difference, "Never done" when no session exists, and "X days ago" for positive day values. Bucket colors must be: green for 1-3 days, orange warning for 4-7 days, red warning for 8+ days. The source-anchor helpers getDaysDiff() and getAdherenceInfo() must be traceable to implementations that own this logic.

- [ ] "Done today" is shown when day difference is 0

- [ ] "X days ago" is shown for positive day differences
- [ ] 1-3 days renders with green styling

- [ ] 8+ days renders with red warning styling
- [ ] getDaysDiff() and getAdherenceInfo() implementations are traceable and own this logic

description:
Source Reference: discovered during 2026-03-21 tracker load-time investigation after pt-wde closure
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: nextjs,tracker,performance,bootstrap,index,agent:codex
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexData.js
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/lib/index-data.js

Issue:
The Next.js tracker should not block the exercise picker on the full history bootstrap payload. Signed-in preview measurements show /api/logs?include_all=true&limit=1000 is the heaviest initial request and delays the first useful tracker surface even though exercise selection only needs exercises and programs first.

Context:
On 2026-03-21, signed-in preview resource timings showed /api/logs?include_all=true&limit=1000 at about 879 ms and ~521 KB decoded, while /api/exercises and /api/programs were only ~320-400 ms and ~53-57 KB. useIndexData currently waits for all three via one Promise.all before setting tracker state, so the picker cannot render until the history payload finishes.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Preserve correctness for logs/history once history finishes loading
- Do not regress offline cache writes or read fallback for tracker bootstrap
- Keep the page orchestrator thin; bootstrap sequencing belongs in hooks/lib
- Avoid broad behavior changes to messaging/history unless directly needed for load-path isolation

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Exercise picker can render from exercises + programs before full history load completes
- [ ] History still loads and populates once the logs request resolves
- [ ] Failed history load does not block exercise selection when exercises/programs succeeded
- [ ] Offline tracker cache behavior is not regressed

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: pt-s81
- parent: pt-eg2
notes:
2026-03-21 implementation update: split tracker bootstrap in useIndexData so exercises + programs resolve and render before the heavier logs payload, while history continues loading in the background with its own non-blocking error state. ExercisePicker now suppresses placeholder adherence badges until history has actually arrived, and README ownership notes were updated for the staged-bootstrap boundary.
2026-03-22 deferred by user to next week so Vercel Analytics / Speed Insights can collect real multi-user, multi-instance tracker data before more route-side load-time tuning. Do not resume this lane before then unless a new production-facing load regression appears.
title: Unblock tracker exercise picker from full history bootstrap

Live signed-in preview verification confirmed tracker:* entries in the performance timeline, including primary-load ~709ms, picker-load ~748ms, and history-load ~1498ms on the sampled reload.

description:
Source Reference: static index parity exploration (2026-03-15)
Status: open
Priority: P1
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex,claude
Tags: migration,ui,index,adherence,time,parity
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/public/index.html
- pt-rebuild/lib/index-history.js
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/components/ExercisePicker.js

Issue:
Next.js index adherence logic computes recency differently from static index, so `Done today` and day-bucket labels can be wrong.

Context:
Static index.html computes recency with getDaysDiff(), which normalizes both dates to local midnight before comparing calendar days. It then uses exact buckets: `0 days = Done today`, `1-3 days = green X day(s) ago`, `4-7 days = orange X days ago`, `8+ days = red X days ago`. Current Next.js lib/index-history.js instead computes `daysSince` as a rolling elapsed-hours floor using `(Date.now() - performed_at) / 24h`, then uses different threshold logic (`0-6 good`, `7-13 due`, `14+ overdue`) before mapping text. This can mislabel sessions around midnight and diverges from the original adherence semantics even when data is correct.

Constraints/Caveats:
Reference behavior is public/index.html getDaysDiff() and getAdherenceInfo(). Preserve the existing ExercisePicker-facing API shape if possible, but the underlying calculation and bucket boundaries must match static parity exactly. Treat local calendar date semantics as functional behavior, not display polish.

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] A session logged before local midnight and viewed after local midnight is not still labeled `Done today`
- [ ] `Done today` is based on local calendar date normalization, not rolling elapsed hours
- [ ] Recency buckets match static exactly: 0, 1-3, 4-7, 8+
- [ ] ExercisePicker badges and adherence text match static index for the same log timestamps

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: pt-flq

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a

Closure Gate:
- Do not close this implementation bead until the linked parity beads (pt-eg2.10, pt-780.6) are verified or closed.
- This bead covers the concrete adherence bug; the linked parity beads remain the final acceptance surface for adherence and time semantics.
notes:
2026-03-18: pt-nyn.1 closed as duplicate. This issue now also covers calendar-day labeling in SessionLoggerModal/useSessionLogging (nyn.1 scope). Fix: extract daysBetween() from lib/rehab-coverage.js into lib/date-utils.js (calendar-day midnight normalization already correct there). Update lib/index-history.js and lib/pt-view.js to import from date-utils.js instead of using rolling 24h math.
Progress update 2026-03-19:
Started the shared calendar-day timing fix described in this issue note.

Change made:
- added lib/date-utils.js with shared local-midnight daysBetween()
- updated lib/index-history.js to use calendar-day semantics and static 0 / 1-3 / 4-7 / 8+ bucket boundaries
- updated lib/pt-view.js to use the same shared daysBetween() for needs-attention recency
- updated lib/rehab-coverage.js to consume the shared helper so migrated date math follows one source of truth
- updated README shared utilities section for the new helper

Validation so far:
- npm run build passed

Remaining:
- parity verification/closure still depends on linked acceptance beads noted on this issue
2026-03-20 implementation-patterns sweep overlap: lib/pt-view.js countRecentSent() still uses Date.now() - 24h rolling math at line ~315. Keep this covered here instead of opening a duplicate date-helper bead.
title: Next.js index adherence uses wrong 'today' and recency bucket rules

close_reason: Fixed in hooks/useAuth.js: when getUser() fails offline, isOfflineSignInError() now guards the sign-out path so a warmed session is trusted from IndexedDB instead of being cleared.
description: When app is opened offline after a prior signed-in session, useAuth calls getUser() which is a network request. Offline, this fails and is treated as an invalid token, signing the user out and leaving a blank screen. Fix: check isOfflineSignInError() before deciding to sign out — if the failure is a network error, trust the locally-stored getSession() result instead.
title: Fix blank screen on offline reopen: trust warmed session without network getUser() call

close_reason: Fixed in commit ba3f286. useAuth.js now calls getUser() after getSession() to validate the token server-side. On error, signs out to clear stale IndexedDB auth_state and lets page fall through to AuthForm.
description:
When a stored Supabase session has an expired access token that cannot be refreshed, getSession() returns the stale session object from IndexedDB storage (authStorage), making session truthy. The page skips AuthForm and renders the data view with broken empty state — all API calls return 401 but nothing converts those failures into a SIGNED_OUT event or sign-in prompt.

Root cause: useAuth.js line 37 — getSession() trusts whatever is in IndexedDB without server-side validation. onAuthStateChange only clears session on explicit SIGNED_OUT event, which never fires if Supabase doesn't attempt a refresh cycle.

Expected behavior: expired/unrefreshable session should result in user seeing sign-in form, not broken empty data state.

Fix options:
1. Handle TOKEN_REFRESH_ERROR in onAuthStateChange → call supabase.auth.signOut() to clear stale session
2. Use getUser() (makes network call) in addition to getSession() on mount — if getUser() returns error, sign out
3. Add 401 response handler in API fetch layer that triggers sign-out

Option 1 is lowest risk: add TOKEN_REFRESH_ERROR case to the onAuthStateChange listener in hooks/useAuth.js — call signOut() to clear the stale IndexedDB session and let the page fall through to AuthForm.

File scope: hooks/useAuth.js
notes:
Root cause refined: not just expired tokens — password reset flow demonstrates the failure mode clearly. updateUser({ password }) invalidates the old session server-side. The subsequent signOut() in reset-password.js is supposed to clear the IndexedDB auth_state, but the authStorage write apparently didn't complete cleanly, leaving an invalidated token in storage. getSession() reads it, session is truthy, AuthForm never shows, all API calls return 401.

This means the bug triggers on any server-side session invalidation: password reset, forced sign-out from another device, admin revocation — not just organic token expiry. More common than originally framed.

Fix priority unchanged (Option 1: TOKEN_REFRESH_ERROR in onAuthStateChange → signOut()), but also need to verify signOut() in reset-password.js actually clears IndexedDB auth_state via authStorage.removeItem. If the async write races with navigation to /, the clear may not complete.
title: Expired session shows empty state instead of re-prompting sign-in

description:
Source Reference: discovered during post-refactor scope review of pt-x2y on 2026-03-21
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: nextjs,cleanup,architecture,pt-view,agent:codex
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/pt-view.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/usePtViewData.js
- pt-rebuild/components/
- pt-rebuild/hooks/
- pt-rebuild/README.md

Issue:
Continue decomposing /pt-view so the page file becomes a true route orchestrator with clearly scoped child components and hooks, rather than a mixed page that still carries too many unrelated responsibilities for future agent work.

Context:
The first cleanup pass in pt-x2y moved the signed-in bootstrap and offline-fallback flow out of pages/pt-view.js and pages/rehab.js. Rehab is now in acceptable orchestrator shape. pt-view is improved but still too broad for AI-maintainable ownership boundaries: the page is 356 lines and still mixes route orchestration with page-local UI-state persistence, note preprocessing/highlighting, inline page-only render sections, message/email glue, and exercise-history modal targeting.

Important discussion context from the 2026-03-21 handoff:
- Do not treat the documented cap as the real stopping rule. Treat content and ownership clarity as the limit.
- The goal is to make future Codex/Claude work as easy and low-risk as possible; smaller, scoped ownership surfaces are preferred over broad mixed files.
- The user's guidance was explicit: what matters is what is best for future agents to manage, debug, edit, and extend, not whether a file is barely under the hard cap.
- Compared with pages/index.js (387 lines, far more subsystems), pt-view at 356 lines is still too large for what it actually owns, which is evidence that its concerns are still too mixed.
- Future-agent rule from discussion: a thin page orchestrator plus scoped hooks/components is better here than asking agents to work inside one 300-400 line mixed page.

Current State / Important Facts:
- Local commit 1b9ebf9 on branch nextjs already landed the first route-orchestrator cleanup for rehab + pt-view and closed pt-x2y locally. That commit is NOT pushed yet.
- Branch state at handoff: nextjs is ahead of origin/nextjs by 1 commit.
- Unrelated in-progress tracker offline edits are still dirty in:
  - pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexData.js
  - pt-rebuild/lib/offline-cache.js
  Leave them alone unless you are explicitly resuming pt-zb3 work.
- Current pt-view responsibilities after pt-x2y:
  - pages/pt-view.js owns auth guard, UI state, modal open/close, inline NeedsAttention/SummaryStats/FiltersPanel renderers, note preprocessing, and message/email glue.
  - hooks/usePtViewData.js owns users/programs/logs bootstrap, patient-context resolution, cache reads/writes, sign-out cache cleanup, and offline fallback.
- Current rehab status after pt-x2y:
  - pages/rehab.js is thin enough for now.
  - hooks/useRehabCoverageData.js owns rehab fetch/cache/fallback and does not need immediate further split.

Best Next Decomposition Targets:
1. Extract page-only render sections from pages/pt-view.js into focused components.
   Recommended first targets:
   - NeedsAttention
   - FiltersPanel
   SummaryStats may stay inline if desired, but can also move if that makes ownership clearer.
2. Move page-local persisted UI-state and note shaping into a focused hook.
   Likely options:
   - hooks/usePtViewUiState.js for filters-expanded, notes-collapsed, dismissed-notes, and IndexedDB UI-state persistence
   - or hooks/usePtViewNotes.js if note preprocessing/highlighting is easier to keep separate from generic UI state
3. Keep pages/pt-view.js focused on:
   - useAuth/usePtViewData/useMessages composition
   - route-level modal open/close state
   - passing props to child components
4. Update README ownership notes in the same change so future agents know where pt-view notes/filter state and page-only panels live.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Do not use page size alone as the stopping rule; use ownership clarity for future agent maintenance as the bar.
- Prefer scoped components/hooks over keeping more mixed logic in pages/pt-view.js.
- Preserve the current profileId fix for useMessages / MessagesModal wiring.
- Preserve current offline fallback behavior from hooks/usePtViewData.js.
- Do not disturb the unrelated dirty tracker offline files unless intentionally switching lanes.
- If you commit this work, include the bead id in the commit message.

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] pages/pt-view.js mostly orchestrates auth, route state, modal state, and composition
- [ ] Inline render helpers that form their own page workspaces/panels are extracted to focused components
- [ ] Persisted UI-state and/or note-shaping logic no longer live broadly in the page file
- [ ] Message viewer/profile id wiring remains correct after refactor
- [ ] README points future agents to the new pt-view ownership boundaries

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: pt-x2y
- related: pt-amc
- blocks: n/a

Resume this when returning to route-structure cleanup or when preparing /pt-view for cutover-readiness review.

notes: 2026-03-13 QA evidence: static /pt_view already has a display-name helper that prefers first_name + last_name and falls back to email prefix at public/pt_view.html:1328-1332, and it resolves the viewing patient name from loaded users at public/pt_view.html:1406-1408. However, static message labels currently still render generic role text at public/pt_view.html:2334 rather than names. On Next.js, the blocker is earlier: pages/pt-view.js fetches the full users list and resolves currentUser/patientUser/currentDbId/recipientId at pages/pt-view.js:158-185, but it does not retain or pass display names into MessagesModal; the modal only receives viewerId and recipientId at pages/pt-view.js:341-346. MessagesModal itself has no label row other than timestamp/read-state metadata at components/MessagesModal.js:148-154. To implement DN-054 cleanly, the agent needs to add name-bearing props/state first, then replace the missing/generic direction labels with first-name-aware labels while keeping null-safe fallbacks.

close_reason: Closed
description:
Source DN: DN-067
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium
Agent: claude
Agent Eligible: yes
Tags: offline, sync, reliability, nextjs, migration
File Scope: pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexOfflineQueue.js

Issue:
App-open while already online does not auto-sync unsynced offline queue entries. The 'online' event listener only fires when the device comes back online mid-session, not on initial mount.

Context:
The offline write queue (pt_offline_queue_${userId}) correctly accumulates sessions when offline and syncs when the 'online' event fires. But if the app is opened fresh while already online with queued items, sync never triggers — entries stay stranded until the user manually refreshes or goes offline/online.

Constraints/Caveats:
Preserve idempotency — sync already uses client_mutation_id and treats 409 as success. Do not re-introduce /api/sync. Queue is user-scoped; must check userId before syncing.

Specific Fix:
File: pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexOfflineQueue.js
In the useEffect that registers the 'online' event listener, add a mount-time sync check:
  - After registering the listener, check if navigator.onLine === true AND queueRef.current.length > 0
  - If so, call sync() immediately
  - This runs once on mount (after queue loads from localStorage), catching stranded entries
Note: queue loads in a separate useEffect dependent on userId — mount-time sync must fire after that useEffect runs. Best approach: add a separate useEffect dependent on [queue, sync] that checks navigator.onLine on the first render once queue is populated.

Validation Checklist:
- Queue items present + app opened online → sync fires on mount, items cleared
- Queue empty + app opened online → no unnecessary sync call
- App opened offline → no sync attempted (navigator.onLine false)
- Online event mid-session still works as before

Dependencies: child of ptrebuild-nyn

Closure Gate:
- Do not close this implementation bead until the linked parity bead (pt-780.3) is verified or closed.
- This bead covers implementation of mount-time sync/reconnect behavior; pt-780.3 remains the final acceptance surface for reconnect parity.
title: [DN-067] Offline resilience parity gap: app-open while already online does not auto-sync unsynced entries, and offline cold-open can fail with "Offline and no cached data available" when cache hydration has not previously occurred.

Apply the identical fix to pt-view.js:

description:
Source Reference: structure audit 2026-03-18
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: low
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex,claude
Tags: nextjs,refactor,structure,lib
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/lib/pt-view.js
- pt-rebuild/lib/users.js (new)
- pt-rebuild/pages/pt-view.js
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js

Issue:
fetchUsers and patchEmailNotifications in lib/pt-view.js are user-management concerns, not pt-view-specific. They must move to lib/users.js before index.js imports them.

Context:
Discovered during structure audit while planning messages wiring (pt-oyv). lib/pt-view.js header says 'pure data logic for the pt-view page' but fetchUsers and patchEmailNotifications are needed by index.js too — cross-page use breaks the page-specific domain claim. The cohesion rule says: if a function is used by more than one page, it does not belong in a page-specific lib.

description:
pages/program.js and pages/rehab.js both link <link rel='manifest' href='/manifest.json'> — this points to the legacy PWA manifest (start_url: /index.html), not the Next.js one.

The correct manifest for Next.js is manifest-tracker.json (start_url: /).

Both files should be updated to: <link rel='manifest' href='/manifest-tracker.json'>

Once this fix lands, manifest.json has no remaining Next.js consumers and becomes prune-eligible as part of pt-v4y.

File scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/program.js
- pt-rebuild/pages/rehab.js

Discovered during dual-agent inventory pass (pt-lvp.2/pt-lvp.3). Claude and Codex agree: manifest.json references in these files are a bug, not intentional. Claude correctly flagged manifest.json as uncertain/bug; Codex treated the references as legitimate consumers (the discrepancy that surfaced the issue).
notes: 2026-03-22 implemented in commit 283974f on nextjs: pages/program.js and pages/rehab.js now reference /manifest-tracker.json instead of the legacy /manifest.json, removing the known wrong-manifest consumer path from active Next.js routes.

description: When a new deploy lands, users on the PWA continue to get old app code from the service worker cache until they close all tabs. Fix: bump the cache version string in sw.js to invalidate old cache, add skipWaiting() so new SW activates immediately, and listen for controllerchange on the client to call window.location.reload(). This makes the Refresh button (or any next page load) pick up new code automatically. Separate from pt-4id (which just restored the Refresh button to the hamburger).
title: SW update: bump cache version + skipWaiting so Refresh picks up new app code

description:

Issue:
After centralizing dosage summary wording, some distance-based exercises render as reps, for example showing 4 x 1 reps instead of 4 x 20 ft. The shared formatter or merge order is allowing fallback rep fields to win when distance should remain authoritative.
Context:
The rebuilt app stores some exercise metadata with fallback rep fields while distance_feet replaces reps for summary display. The new shared helper merged exercise and program data, and a regression was reported immediately after rollout: distance now shows as reps on at least one surface.
Constraints/Caveats:
- Preserve the approved dosage grammar from pt-l8q7
- Do not change stored dosage values
- Keep distance and duration as reps-replacing summaries
Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Distance dosage renders as S x D ft
- [ ] Sided distance dosage renders as S x D ft per side
- [ ] Rep dosage still renders as S x R reps
- [ ] Hold and duration summaries remain correct
Dependencies:
- discovered-from: pt-l8q7
- blocks: n/a
Reactivation Trigger:
Reopen if any prescribed dosage summary still shows fallback reps where distance or duration should replace reps.
notes:
Root cause confirmed in shared dosage-summary helper: plain object spread let null program fields overwrite valid exercise metadata from /index, so distance_feet and dosage_type could disappear while current_reps stayed 1. Fix changed the helper to resolve each field by first non-null value instead of raw object spread. Added regression test covering a program row with reps=1 plus null distance metadata and an exercise with distance dosage metadata.
Reopened after preview verification on 2026-03-23. Playwright on both the branch alias preview and the latest deployment still renders Monster Walk as 4 x 1 reps and Side Steps as 2 x 1 reps per side, so the prior fix did not resolve the live data/render path.
Live preview/API investigation confirms the remaining distance-summary bug is caused by migrated patient_programs data, not the shared formatter. Evidence: /api/programs preview response returns Side Steps and Monster Walk with dosage_type='reps', reps_per_set=1, distance_feet=null while nested exercise metadata still has pattern_modifiers=['distance_feet']. Root cause source found in db/migrate.js:299-304, where migratePrograms() writes dosage_type: ex.current.type and distance_feet: ex.current.distance || null. Legacy archive shape stores the real distance on ex.current.distanceFeet (camelCase), not ex.current.distance. In PT_Backup/archive/pt_all_data-20260118.json, Side Steps current spec includes type='reps', sets=2, repsPerSet=1, distanceFeet=20 and Monster Walk includes type='reps', sets=4, repsPerSet=1, distanceFeet=20. That mismatch caused Jan 18 migrated patient_programs rows to preserve the placeholder rep data while dropping distance_feet, which is why /index still renders 2 x 1 reps per side and 4 x 1 reps on preview. Next step should be a data repair/backfill (and migrate.js correction for future imports), not another UI wording change.
Live preview/API investigation confirms the remaining distance-summary bug is caused by migrated patient_programs data, not the shared formatter. Evidence: /api/programs preview response returns Side Steps and Monster Walk with dosage_type='reps', reps_per_set=1, distance_feet=null while nested exercise metadata still has pattern_modifiers=['distance_feet']. Root cause source found in db/migrate.js:299-304, where migratePrograms() writes dosage_type: ex.current.type and distance_feet: ex.current.distance || null. In PT_Backup/archive/pt_all_data-20260118.json, Side Steps current spec includes type='reps', sets=2, repsPerSet=1, distanceFeet=20 and Monster Walk includes type='reps', sets=4, repsPerSet=1, distanceFeet=20. That mismatch caused Jan 18 migrated patient_programs rows to preserve the placeholder rep data while dropping distance_feet, which is why /index still renders 2 x 1 reps per side and 4 x 1 reps on preview. Next step should be a data repair/backfill (and migrate.js correction for future imports), not another UI wording change.
Implemented repo-side repair: db/migrate.js now reads legacy current.distanceFeet before current.distance for patient_program imports, and added supabase/migrations/20260324010000_backfill_distance_programs.sql to backfill Side Steps and Monster Walk program rows to dosage_type=distance, distance_feet=20, reps_per_set=null when they still have the legacy reps=1 placeholder shape.
Removed the earlier Firebase-import script change after user confirmed the import path is permanently retired. Remaining repo-side fix is the one-time Supabase backfill migration only: supabase/migrations/20260324010000_backfill_distance_programs.sql.

Pushed commit bec4d7f to origin/nextjs. Re-ran live Supabase backup after push (backup-2026-03-24_00-51-32.json); Side Steps and Monster Walk patient_programs rows remain unchanged with dosage_type='reps', reps_per_set=1, distance_feet=null. This confirms pushing the repo/migration file did not apply the DB backfill automatically.
Attempting direct remote migration apply via Supabase CLI using PT_Backup/supabase.env and SUPABASE_DB_URL after confirming project ref zvgoaxdpkgfxklotqwpz is linked.
Supabase CLI reached the linked project successfully. Current blocker is migration history drift: remote has applied versions not present under local supabase/migrations. Proceeding with supabase migration fetch --linked to align local migration files with remote history before retrying db push.
Fetched missing remote migration files into local supabase/migrations so history now matches the linked project. Retrying supabase db push --linked to apply 20260324010000_backfill_distance_programs.sql.
supabase db push completed through the migration file path after fetching remote history. Verifying against fresh live backup backup-2026-03-24_01-05-28.json.
Committing the fetched remote migration-history files so local supabase/migrations matches the linked project and future db push calls do not fail on history drift.

description:
Source Reference: none (discovered during offline investigation, reported by user 2026-03-13)
Status: open
Priority: P1
Risk: medium — affects all pages, every navigation
Scope: Static app confirmed. Next.js unverified — must check before implementing.
Agent Owner: claude
Agent Eligible: yes
Tags: auth, offline, flash, ux, nextjs, migration, reliability
File Scope: pt-rebuild/hooks/useAuth.js (Next.js), pt-rebuild/public/index.html (static)

Issue:
Two related auth problems (observed on static app; Next.js occurrence unverified):
1. (Online) Sign-in modal briefly flashes on app open and when navigating between pages, then disappears. Occurs even when the user is authenticated.
2. (Offline) Sign-in modal appears when app is opened offline and does not dismiss. Entering credentials shows 'Offline - no cached version available'.

IMPORTANT — Verification required before implementing:
User confirmed this was observed on the static app (public/index.html). It may not occur in the Next.js version. Before writing any code, verify on the Next.js preview (https://pt-rehab-git-nextjs-pt-tracker.vercel.app):
  - Open app while signed in — does AuthForm flash?
  - Navigate between /rehab, /pt-view, /program — does AuthForm flash?
  - Open app offline — does AuthForm stay up and block access?
If none of these reproduce on Next.js, this is a static-only bug and should be moved to dev_notes.json (non-NextJS tracker).

Context:
Root cause (online flash, Next.js theory): useAuth.js onAuthStateChange unconditionally calls setSession(sess). If sess is momentarily null during a Supabase token refresh cycle and loading is already false, pages with !session && !authLoading show AuthForm briefly.

Root cause (offline, Next.js theory): supabase.auth.getSession() attempts to refresh an expired/near-expired token via network. When offline, this refresh fails, getSession() returns null session, loading is set to false, and all pages show AuthForm.

Supabase stores session in localStorage: sb-zvgoaxdpkgfxklotqwpz-auth-token.

Constraints/Caveats:
- If confirmed on Next.js: fix useAuth.js only (no page-level changes needed).
- If static-only: move to dev_notes.json and out of Beads.
- Do not show AuthForm when offline and a stored session exists.
- Do not call supabase.auth.signInWithPassword offline.

Specific Fix (if Next.js confirmed):
File: pt-rebuild/hooks/useAuth.js

Fix 1 (online flash): In onAuthStateChange, only set session to null on SIGNED_OUT:
  supabase.auth.onAuthStateChange((event, sess) => {
      if (event === 'SIGNED_OUT') { setSession(null); }
      else if (sess) { setSession(sess); }
  });

Fix 2 (offline fallback): If getSession() returns null and !navigator.onLine, read stored session from localStorage key sb-zvgoaxdpkgfxklotqwpz-auth-token and use it as fallback. Expose isOffline: boolean from hook.

Validation Checklist:
- Open app signed in → no AuthForm flash (online)
- Navigate between pages signed in → no AuthForm flash
- Open app offline (session stored) → tracker loads, no AuthForm
- Open app offline (never signed in) → AuthForm shown (correct)
- Sign out → AuthForm shown correctly
- Back online after offline session → fresh token loaded

Dependencies: discovered-from ptrebuild-zb3, child of ptrebuild-nyn
notes: 2026-03-17 User confirmed: offline behavior is the active bug on Next.js. Repro steps: sign in → go offline → close app → immediately reopen → sign-in window appears and will not dismiss (can't log in offline). Online flash: not observed on Next.js preview (loaded cleanly on hard reload, no console auth errors). Offline fix is the priority. Root cause: getSession() attempts token refresh over network; offline refresh fails; returns null session; loading=false; app shows AuthForm. Fix: useAuth.js — (1) only setSession(null) on SIGNED_OUT event; (2) if getSession() returns null and navigator.onLine===false, read localStorage key sb-zvgoaxdpkgfxklotqwpz-auth-token as fallback.
title: Auth sign-in modal flashes on app open and page navigation; fails when offline

2026-03-13 Beads process feedback for later keep/change decision:
- Likes: parent/child issue structure worked well for narrowing parity work without bloating the main 4c/4d tasks; closure-block notes made the final state easy to recover; Beads history was useful for reconstructing prior verification context.
- Gripes: auto-push metadata churn can leave the local Dolt working set dirty even when the actual issue content is synced; this can block bd dolt pull with a confusing 'cannot merge with uncommitted changes' message until the metadata table is committed. Concurrent writers also make auto-push non-fast-forward failures fairly common.
- Operational lesson: for parallel sessions, implementation should stay single-threaded on shared files while review/verification can happen in separate threads; Beads updates are safest when one thread owns the active issue subtree.
- Revisit later with Claude: decide whether current Beads + Dolt auto-push behavior is worth keeping as-is, or whether the workflow should standardize a clearer sync/commit step when multiple agents are active.

description:
Source Reference: static index.html parity rule reported by user on 2026-03-16
Status: open
Priority: P1
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex,claude
Tags: nextjs,index,parity,datetime,logging,bug
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js
- pt-rebuild/components/SessionLoggerModal.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useSessionLogging.js
- pt-rebuild/public/index.html

Issue:
The static index already applies a local calendar-day rule for date/time labeling and tracking, but the Next.js build has not preserved that behavior. The effective day must be determined by the local date boundary at midnight, not by whether the timestamp is within the last 24 hours.

Context:
This is a static-parity carryover bug, not a new product rule. Current Next.js behavior appears to use a rolling 24-hour window instead of local calendar-day boundaries. Example: at 10:00 AM today, an entry logged at 4:00 PM yesterday is incorrectly shown as "today". Static behavior explicitly treats that entry as yesterday because its local calendar date is yesterday.

Expected Behavior:
- If the logged local date is today, show today.
- If the logged local date is yesterday, show yesterday, even if fewer than 24 hours have elapsed.
- Do not classify entries by elapsed hours alone.
- Use the same effective-day rule anywhere index logging, history summaries, or adherence/status labeling depend on the day of the session.

Constraints/Caveats:
Preserve local-time semantics. Do not introduce UTC/date-shift regressions in datetime-local handling. Verify against the explicit static index implementation rather than inferring behavior from current Next.js output. If helper logic is shared with history or adherence calculations, keep the rule consistent across those consumers.

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] At 10:00 AM today, an entry logged at 4:00 PM yesterday is shown/labeled as yesterday
- [ ] Logging an entry before local midnight stores/displays it as yesterday after midnight passes
- [ ] Logging after midnight still maps to the current local day
- [ ] Datetime-local roundtrip does not shift the intended local date due to timezone conversion
- [ ] History/adherence or related index summaries use the same explicit static day rule

Dependencies:
- parent-child: pt-nyn

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a
notes:
2026-03-16 clarification from user:
The current Next.js behavior appears to use a rolling 24-hour window instead of local calendar-day boundaries. Example: at 10:00 AM today, an entry logged at 4:00 PM yesterday is being shown as "today". Static parity should use calendar-day semantics instead: anything before local midnight belongs to yesterday, even if it is less than 24 hours old.
Static source reference:
- public/index.html:2175-2181 defines `getDaysDiff()` using local calendar-day boundaries by zeroing both dates to local midnight before computing day difference.
- public/index.html:2199-2208 uses that local-calendar `daysSince` value in `getAdherenceInfo()` and labels `daysSince === 0` as "Done today".
This is the source-of-truth behavior the Next.js build needs to preserve.
title: Port static calendar-day labeling rule for index logging

The shared auth UI should tell the user that signing in requires an internet connection, and that reopening offline only works when a session was already saved on this device.

- Preserve warmed-session offline reopen behavior; do not clear or bypass stored sessions

- Apply the fix in the shared auth path so /, /program, /pt-view, and /rehab stay consistent

Status: open

Issue:
Continue decomposing /pt-view so the page file becomes a true route orchestrator with clearly scoped child components and hooks, rather than a mixed page that still carries too many unrelated responsibilities for future agent work.

Context:
The first cleanup pass in pt-x2y moved the signed-in bootstrap and offline-fallback flow out of pages/pt-view.js and pages/rehab.js. Rehab is now in acceptable orchestrator shape. pt-view is improved but still too broad for AI-maintainable ownership boundaries: the page is 356 lines and still mixes route orchestration with page-local UI-state persistence, note preprocessing/highlighting, inline page-only render sections, message/email glue, and exercise-history modal targeting. Compared with pages/index.js (387 lines, far more subsystems) and the project guidance that pages should mainly compose hooks/components, pt-view still needs another decomposition pass before cutover.

Current State / Important Facts:
- Local commit 1b9ebf9 on branch nextjs already landed the first route-orchestrator cleanup for rehab + pt-view and closed pt-x2y locally. That commit is NOT pushed yet.
- Branch state at handoff: nextjs is ahead of origin/nextjs by 1 commit.
- Unrelated in-progress tracker offline edits are still dirty in:
  - pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexData.js
  - pt-rebuild/lib/offline-cache.js
  Leave them alone unless you are explicitly resuming pt-zb3 work.
- Current pt-view responsibilities after pt-x2y:
  - pages/pt-view.js owns auth guard, UI state, modal open/close, inline NeedsAttention/SummaryStats/FiltersPanel renderers, note preprocessing, and message/email glue.
  - hooks/usePtViewData.js owns users/programs/logs bootstrap, patient-context resolution, cache reads/writes, sign-out cache cleanup, and offline fallback.
- Current rehab status after pt-x2y:
  - pages/rehab.js is thin enough for now.
  - hooks/useRehabCoverageData.js owns rehab fetch/cache/fallback and does not need immediate further split.

Best Next Decomposition Targets:
1. Extract page-only render sections from pages/pt-view.js into focused components.
   Recommended first targets:
   - NeedsAttention
   - FiltersPanel
   SummaryStats may stay inline if desired, but can also move if that makes ownership clearer.
2. Move page-local persisted UI-state and note shaping into a focused hook.
   Likely options:
   - hooks/usePtViewUiState.js for filters-expanded, notes-collapsed, dismissed-notes, and IndexedDB UI-state persistence
   - or hooks/usePtViewNotes.js if note preprocessing/highlighting is easier to keep separate from generic UI state
3. Keep pages/pt-view.js focused on:
   - useAuth/usePtViewData/useMessages composition
   - route-level modal open/close state
   - passing props to child components
4. Update README ownership notes in the same change so future agents know where pt-view notes/filter state and page-only panels live.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Do not use page size alone as the stopping rule; use ownership clarity for future agent maintenance as the bar.
- Prefer scoped components/hooks over keeping more mixed logic in pages/pt-view.js.
- Preserve the current profileId fix for useMessages / MessagesModal wiring.
- Preserve current offline fallback behavior from hooks/usePtViewData.js.
- Do not disturb the unrelated dirty tracker offline files unless intentionally switching lanes.
- If you commit this work, include the bead id in the commit message.

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] pages/pt-view.js mostly orchestrates auth, route state, modal state, and composition
- [ ] Inline render helpers that form their own page workspaces/panels are extracted to focused components
- [ ] Persisted UI-state and/or note-shaping logic no longer live broadly in the page file
- [ ] Message viewer/profile id wiring remains correct after refactor
- [ ] README points future agents to the new pt-view ownership boundaries

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: pt-x2y
- related: pt-amc
- blocks: n/a

Reactivation Trigger:
Resume this when returning to route-structure cleanup or when preparing /pt-view for cutover-readiness review.
title: Further decompose pt-view for AI-manageable ownership boundaries

description:
Source Reference: user report 2026-03-19
Status: open
Priority: P1
Risk: medium
Scope: current
Agent Owner: shared
Agent Eligible: codex,claude
Tags: nextjs,offline,sync,reliability,queue
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useTrackerSession.js
- pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexOfflineQueue.js
- pt-rebuild/lib/index-offline.js

Issue:
A newly saved tracker session can remain unsynced until the user manually triggers Sync, because the immediate post-save sync attempt can run before the newly enqueued queue item is visible to the sync path.

Context:
User report on 2026-03-19: after logging an exercise, the session did not sync until manual Sync was triggered from the hamburger menu.
Current code path review:
- hooks/useTrackerSession.js enqueues the payload, then immediately calls sync()
- hooks/useIndexOfflineQueue.js sync() reads queueRef.current
- queueRef.current can still reflect the pre-enqueue queue on the same tick, so the just-added session may be skipped by that immediate sync attempt
This appears distinct from the already tracked mount-time auto-sync-on-open issue and from the existing sync-toast/copy parity bead.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Preserve queue-first save semantics
- Do not regress duplicate-safe 409 handling
- Do not require the user to trigger manual Sync for a newly saved online session
- Keep user-scoped queue behavior intact

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Immediately after queueing a newly saved session while online, the sync path includes that newly queued item
- [ ] Newly saved online sessions do not remain stranded until manual Sync
- [ ] Queue-first save order is preserved (enqueue before network attempt)
- [ ] 409 duplicate responses still drain the queued item successfully
- [ ] User-scoped queue storage behavior remains intact

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: n/a
- blocks: n/a

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a
notes: 2026-03-20 session handoff: code fix remains in place for immediate post-save sync including the just-enqueued payload, but this bead still needs end-to-end verification on preview/device before closure. If revisiting tracker offline work next session, verify this alongside pt-zb3/pt-41a index offline-read testing so sync behavior is checked in the same pass.
title: Fix immediate post-save sync missing newly queued tracker sessions

description:
Source DN: DN-049
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: low
Agent: codex

Issue:
Phase 4d: Port rep counter, hold timer, duration timer, and voice cues from public/index.html into TimerPanel + useTimerSpeech hook. Web Audio API (iOS-safe beep). Web Speech API for voice cues.

File scope:
pt-rebuild/components/TimerPanel.js, pt-rebuild/components/TimerPanel.module.css, pt-rebuild/hooks/useTimerSpeech.js

Context:
Part of Phase 4 index.html migration (DN-045). TimerPanel receives exercise and set context as props. useTimerSpeech manages timer state and speech synthesis. Claude verifies iOS audio + speech behavior on preview.

Constraints/Caveats:
No onClick. onPointerUp only. 44px targets. Web Audio API must use AudioContext resume() for iOS autoplay policy. Speech synthesis must handle iOS quirks (utterance must be triggered from user gesture).

Closure Gate:
- Do not close this implementation bead until the linked parity beads (pt-spg.2, pt-spg.3, pt-spg.7, pt-spg.10) are verified or closed.
- This bead covers TimerPanel and speech implementation work; the linked parity beads remain the final acceptance surface for counter, timer, Pocket Mode, and speech/copy parity.
notes:
2026-03-06: Implemented TimerPanel/useTimerSpeech and wired index exercise tap to open TimerPanel. Added countdown beeps (3/2/1), completion triple-beep, and speech cues (start/pause/milestones/set complete). Build passes locally. Commit: 46ef84c.
2026-03-13: Codex fixed TimerPanel state reset/parity issues: reopening the same exercise now resets timer/counter state, hold mode tracks completed reps explicitly, and 'Use This Value' stays disabled until the timer/counter has actually recorded something. Local next build passes after the change.
2026-03-13: Production parity check on https://pttracker.app using the real patient account confirms 4d entry behavior. Plain reps exercises do NOT open SessionLoggerModal directly; they open the same in-panel exercise logger used for hold exercises. Example: 'Ankle Inversion (TheraBand)' opened a panel with side selector, big tappable rep counter, Undo, Previous / Log Set / Next Set / Done / Pocket Mode / Back to Exercises. Hold exercise 'Ankle Inversion — Isometric' opened the same panel with timer controls added (Rep 1 of 10, countdown, Start/Reset, same lower controls). Pocket Mode is part of this same 4d flow: it opens a full-screen overlay, shows countdown/meta text for timer exercises, and updates hint text from 'Tap to start' to 'Tap to pause · Hold for partial' while running. This means current Next.js behavior is still behind production parity: TimerPanel needs to be the entry path for both reps and hold/duration exercises, and Pocket Mode needs explicit overlay behavior rather than being omitted.
2026-03-13: Production check on https://pttracker.app with the real patient account confirmed that reps-only exercises and hold exercises both open the same in-panel logger. Reps example: 'Ankle Inversion (TheraBand)' opened side selector + tappable rep counter + Previous/Log Set/Next Set/Done/Pocket Mode/Back controls. Hold example: 'Ankle Inversion — Isometric' opened the same panel with timer controls plus those lower controls. Pocket Mode is part of 4d and is a full-screen overlay; for hold exercises it shows countdown/meta text and changes hint text from 'Tap to start' to 'Tap to pause · Hold for partial' while running.
2026-03-13: Next.js branch now routes all exercise taps into TimerPanel to match production panel-first behavior, and the timer state machine was trimmed back to the structure cap while preserving build-green behavior.
2026-03-13 parity review findings still open in 4d: (1) Pocket Mode does not yet reflect real session progress or side-aware progress; current Next.js panel/overlay still uses placeholder-style counts instead of session-backed left/right and sets-left state. (2) Pocket Mode partial-hold handling diverged from static semantics because Next.js set patches were carrying partial_rep=true; static logger keeps partial_rep false in the persisted set payload. These findings stay under 4d because they are part of the timer/pocket execution flow.
2026-03-13: Closed child ptrebuild-f1j after preview verification on https://pt-rehab-git-nextjs-pt-tracker.vercel.app. Verified Pocket Mode now reflects session-backed progress, reps and hold exercises both stay panel-first, and a captured hold payload after Pocket Mode long-press logging showed manual_log=false and partial_rep=false. ptrebuild-ryf remains open pending broader audio/speech parity confidence, but the panel/pocket parity slice is complete.
2026-03-17 active refactor progress: logger timer/cue behavior is being moved into the new expandable machine core at pt-rebuild/lib/logger-timer-machine.js. Counter cues, side-change speech, timer start/pause/reset/tick/completion, pocket partial cue, Pocket Mode context, and duration-completion speech shaping are now routing through the machine/adapters rather than staying as scattered inline rules. next build passes after the current slice; ptrebuild-ryf still stays open pending parity verification and any remaining cue-alignment cleanup.
2026-03-18 implementation progress: timer/audio work is no longer blocked on unresolved comparison behavior. Delayed comparison speech is now wired into the Next Set confirmation flow using the approved best-set/total-reps model and prior-session cutoff, so the remaining work under this bead is runtime verification and any small parity cleanup rather than missing architecture.
2026-03-18 restart handoff: timer/audio architecture is no longer blocked on unresolved comparison behavior. The shared machine core is in place and comparison speech is wired into the Next Set confirmation flow using the approved best-set/total-reps model with prior-session cutoff. The remaining work under this bead is runtime verification and any small parity cleanup, not missing architecture. Working tree currently includes modified TimerPanel/useExerciseTimer/useTimerSpeech/useTimerAudio/usePanelSessionProgress/pages/index plus new lib/logger-timer-machine.js and lib/logger-progress-comparison.js. Rerun next build after restart, then verify pt-9v1 child-bead behavior and commit.
title: [DN-049] Phase 4d: Port rep counter, hold timer, duration timer, and voice cues from public/index.html into TimerPanel + useTimerSpeech hook. Web Audio API (iOS-safe beep). Web Speech API for voice cues.

description: Source Reference: discovered during deployed preview verification on 2026-03-17. Issue: sided exercises currently show visual copy Working left side / Working right side in TimerPanel, but the machine only emits that cue on SELECT_SIDE and not when a sided exercise first opens. Next.js should speak the initial working-side cue on logger open so the first spoken side context matches the visual state and later side-change behavior. File Scope: pt-rebuild/lib/logger-timer-machine.js, pt-rebuild/hooks/useTimerSpeech.js, pt-rebuild/components/TimerPanel.js. Dependencies: related ptrebuild-spg.10, discovered-from ptrebuild-ryf.

The spec requires these exact visible states: empty-state copy "No messages yet. The "Hide" affordance archives the message; "Undo Send" is a destructive action limited to the recent undo window.

- [ ] Incoming messages show sender label "PT → You"

The checkbox state is synced from currentEmailNotifyEnabled when the messages modal opens, not re-fetched at that point.

Non-blocking enhancement items were deferred separately (initial side-open speech cue and richer duration-side phrasing). Items not explicitly covered by this note still need separate confirmation if this parent remains open, including broader copy/state items such as All sets complete, Rep N complete midpoint feedback, exact save feedback strings, and any global stale-speech queue cases outside the verified timer/audio slice.

Current code now covers: `All sets complete` via hooks/useLoggerFeedback.js after session-progress completion check; `Rep N complete` midpoint feedback in components/TimerPanel.js for hold progression before final completion; exact `Saved (with notes)` / `Saved (no notes)` success copy via hooks/useLoggerFeedback.js and pages/index.js. description:
Source Reference: nextjs-parity-governance
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium

Agent Eligible: codex
Tags: nextjs,migration,parity,agent:codex,parity-logging
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js

Issue:
Verify that timer mode counts down from target, hold mode advances reps within a set, duration mode is single-run, and the five-part surface is preserved.

Context:
Spec: pt-rebuild/openspec/changes/nextjs-parity-governance/specs/tracker-logging-and-pocket-parity/spec.md
Timer mode must count down (not up) from the configured target. Hold exercises: each completed countdown rep advances live rep progress, resets the timer display to full target duration, auto-pauses on completion, and defers set acceptance until the configured rep count is satisfied or the user manually logs. Duration exercises: one countdown run produces one rep with captured seconds — single-run semantics, not multi-rep. The five-part timer surface must include: rep/duration info line, timer display, target label, start/pause button, and reset button. Reset clears live timer progress without deleting accepted sets.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code
- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec
- Mark done only after all Validation Checklist items pass
- Do not introduce new behavior not described in the spec

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Timer counts down from the configured target, not up
- [ ] Timer surface has five named parts: rep/duration info line, timer display, target label, start/pause button, reset button
- [ ] Hold: completed countdown rep auto-pauses and advances live rep count without appending an accepted set
- [ ] Hold: timer display resets to full target duration after each rep completes
- [ ] Hold: set is not accepted until target rep count is satisfied or user manually logs
- [ ] Duration: one countdown run produces one rep with captured seconds (single-run, not multi-rep hold)
- [ ] Timer reset clears live timer progress only — accepted sets in currentSession.sets are unaffected

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: n/a
- blocks: n/a

n/a

Remaining open scope on this parent, if any, is broader logger/timer acceptance outside the now-closed timer/audio cue slice.
2026-03-22 reconciliation note: this child's own 2026-03-18 note already marks every checklist item here as satisfied by the completed timer/audio implementation and verification work under pt-ryf plus closed child beads pt-9v1.2 through pt-9v1.7. showNextSetModal() summarizes the currently captured live value against the configured target and the active side when applicable. If reps are 0 and no seconds are captured, save is blocked with "Please enter a value greater than 0". The confirmed payload must include: set_number, normalized reps and seconds, distance_feet: null, the active main-tracker side, optional normalized form_data, manual_log: false, partial_rep: false, and a fresh performed_at timestamp. After confirming a reps set, the visible counter resets to 0 immediately and comparison speech fires delayed (not in the same instant as acceptance).

Constraints/Caveats:
- Read the canonical spec file before writing or verifying any code
- Do not change behavior to match assumptions — verify against the spec

finishSession() blocks with "Please log at least one set before finishing" until at least one accepted set exists. The notes modal is opened (not direct save). Session will be logged at the selected time." close-notes-modal returns to the active logger without discarding the session. cancel-session asks for confirmation before abandoning. Confirmed discard clears both currentSession and currentExercise. Final save: trim notes → apply backdate (rewrites session timestamp and each set's performed_at) → push to queue → update sync badge → attempt network sync → prepend to local history → clear tracker state → return to picker → re-render adherence → show "Saved (with notes)" or "Saved (no notes)".
