notes:
Exercise archived/lifecycle_status inventory for scoped .js files in pt-rebuild/.
Search scope: all .js files, excluding node_modules, .next, .serena, and db/migrate.js.
Method: direct exercise-field references only. Excluded unrelated message archival usage and comment/UI-copy-only mentions.

Live exercise-field hits
- api/roles.js:27 — reads archived — `archived` included in `exercises!inner (...)` select list.
- api/roles.js:31 — reads archived — `.eq('exercises.archived', false)` filters out archived exercises.
- components/ExerciseForm.js:11 — writes archived — initializes new exercise form state with `archived: false`.
- components/ExerciseForm.js:57 — reads archived — seeds edit form state from `exercise.archived`.
- components/ExerciseForm.js:120 — writes lifecycle_status — sends `lifecycle.status` in save payload as `lifecycle_status`.
- components/ExerciseRolesWorkspace.js:40 — reads archived — labels archived exercises in selector options.
- components/ProgramDosageWorkspace.js:46 — reads archived — labels archived exercises in selector options.
- components/ProgramExerciseSelector.js:40 — reads archived — labels archived exercises in selector options.
- hooks/useProgramWorkspaceState.js:7 — reads archived — filters archived exercises out when `showArchived` is off.
- hooks/useProgramWorkspaceState.js:16 — reads archived — hides archived exercises from workspace options unless already selected.
- hooks/usePtViewData.js:61 — reads archived — filters cached programs whose joined `exercises` row is archived.
- hooks/usePtViewData.js:82 — reads archived — same filter on freshly fetched programs.
- lib/exercise-sort.js:88 — reads archived — excludes archived exercises from visible ordered results.
- lib/handlers/exercises.js:90 — reads archived — copies DB row field `ex.archived` into assembled exercise object.
- lib/handlers/exercises.js:91 — reads lifecycle_status — checks `ex.lifecycle_status` when building normalized lifecycle object.
- lib/handlers/exercises.js:92 — reads lifecycle_status — maps DB field to `lifecycle.status`.
- lib/handlers/exercises.js:236 — reads lifecycle_status — validates incoming payload field.
- lib/handlers/exercises.js:346 — reads archived — destructures incoming create payload with default `false`.
- lib/handlers/exercises.js:353 — reads lifecycle_status — destructures incoming create payload with default `null`.
- lib/handlers/exercises.js:399 — writes archived — inserts `archived` into `exercises` row.
- lib/handlers/exercises.js:400 — writes lifecycle_status — inserts `lifecycle_status` into `exercises` row.
- lib/handlers/exercises.js:528 — reads archived — destructures incoming update payload.
- lib/handlers/exercises.js:535 — reads lifecycle_status — destructures incoming update payload.
- lib/handlers/exercises.js:583 — writes archived — applies direct archived update to DB update object.
- lib/handlers/exercises.js:584 — reads lifecycle_status — branches on presence of update payload field.
- lib/handlers/exercises.js:585 — writes lifecycle_status — applies lifecycle status to DB update object.
- lib/handlers/exercises.js:587 — writes archived — derives archived boolean from lifecycle status during update.
- lib/handlers/exercises.js:760 — writes archived — soft-delete path updates exercise to `archived: true`.
- lib/handlers/programs.js:200 — reads archived — includes joined exercise `archived` field in program query.
- lib/program-optimistic.js:19 — reads archived — builds optimistic exercise object from payload field.
- lib/program-optimistic.js:26 — reads lifecycle_status — checks payload field when building optimistic lifecycle object.
- lib/program-optimistic.js:27 — reads lifecycle_status — maps payload field to optimistic `lifecycle.status`.
- lib/pt-view.js:171 — reads archived — skips overdue calculations for programs whose joined exercise is archived.

Excluded as non-operational / not direct exercise-field reads-writes
- lib/handlers/exercises.js:37 — comment mentions `lifecycle_status`.
- lib/handlers/exercises.js:38 — lifecycle status vocabulary includes `'archived'`, but this is not archived-boolean field access.
- components/ExerciseFormCore.js:85 — JSDoc mentions `archived`.
- components/ExerciseFormLifecycle.js:7 — lifecycle status option list includes `'archived'`.
- components/ExerciseFormLifecycle.js:56 — UI copy says `archived`.

Summary counts
- reads archived: 17
- writes archived: 6
- reads lifecycle_status: 8
- writes lifecycle_status: 3

Cross-check result
- Independent subagent pass matched this inventory with no extra live exercise-field hits.
DIRECTION DECISION (pending pt-t1qr.1 results): Preferred approach is a Supabase DB trigger, NOT a JS codebase sweep. Trigger: BEFORE UPDATE on exercises, sets archived = (lifecycle_status = 'archived'). Rationale: (1) minimal code changes, (2) boolean index stays efficient for read-heavy app (app drains ~0.4%/min battery — keep reads fast), (3) .eq() on boolean beats .neq() on string for query planner, (4) trigger fires only on writes (archiving is rare). Wait for pt-t1qr.1 search results before implementing — need to confirm no code is writing archived directly in a way that would conflict with the trigger. Also need to confirm how deprecated exercises are filtered (suspected lifecycle_status filter already exists).
title: Replace archived boolean reads with lifecycle_status throughout codebase

### What This Is
One paragraph: PT tracker app for physical therapy session logging. Two users (admin/therapist). Deployed on Vercel + Supabase.

### Two Structures in This Folder
Honest explanation: legacy vanilla JS pages live in public/ and api/; Next.js migration lives in pages/, components/, hooks/. Both are active. The migration is incremental (strangler fig) — old HTML pages are retired one at a time as Next.js versions are verified. Branch: nextjs. See docs/NEXTJS_MIGRATION.md for migration status.

npm run dev → http://localhost:3000

Annotated tree of pt-rebuild/ — what each top-level folder and key file is for. Cover: pages/, components/, hooks/, lib/, api/, public/, styles/, supabase/, docs/. Do NOT invent structure — read the actual filesystem.

Registry pulled from the Shared Architecture section of docs/NEXTJS_MIGRATION.md. For each component: file name, what it does, when to use it, basic usage. Components to document: NavMenu.js, AuthForm.js, NativeSelect.js, ExercisePicker.js, SessionLoggerModal.js (and any others found in components/).

### Shared Utilities (lib/)
Same treatment. Lib files to document: supabase.js, text-format.js, rehab-coverage.js, and any others found in lib/ that are Next.js-layer files. Do NOT document lib/auth.js or lib/db.js here — those are legacy API-layer files, note them separately as 'Legacy API layer (do not use in Next.js pages)'.

### Shared Hooks (hooks/)
Same treatment. useAuth.js, and any others in hooks/.

### Key Docs
Bullet list pointing to: docs/NEXTJS_MIGRATION.md (migration status + decisions), docs/NEXTJS_STRUCTURE.md (authoring rules for new files), docs/AGENTS.md (workflow rules for agents), docs/BEADS_TEMPLATE.md (issue tracker template).

### Deployment
Vercel project: pt-rehab. Production: https://pttracker.app. Preview (nextjs branch): https://pt-rehab-git-nextjs-pt-tracker.vercel.app. Supabase project: zvgoaxdpkgfxklotqwpz.

description: Purpose: shared operational issue sink for Codex/Claude when tooling/process friction reduces delivery speed or reliability (Beads runtime issues, merge/handoff pain, tracker drift, branch hygiene friction, CI friction). Rules: (1) create discovered-from child issues for concrete incidents, (2) include root cause + mitigation, (3) close child after fix, (4) keep epic open as longitudinal signal for whether Beads remains best-fit. Scope: agent operations only, not product features.
notes:
2026-03-13 Beads process feedback from active multi-agent migration work:
- Likes: parent/child issue structure worked well for carving off parity slices without bloating the main tasks; closure-block notes made recovery easy; Beads history was useful for reconstructing prior verification context.
- Gripes: auto-push metadata churn can leave the local Dolt working set dirty even when the actual issue content is already synced, which then blocks bd dolt pull with a 'cannot merge with uncommitted changes' message until metadata is committed. Concurrent writers also make non-fast-forward auto-push failures common.
- Operational lesson: implementation should stay single-threaded on shared files while review/verification can run in parallel threads; Beads updates are safest when one thread owns the active issue subtree.
- Decision input for later: revisit whether current Beads + Dolt auto-push behavior is worth keeping as-is, or whether the workflow should standardize a clearer sync/commit step when multiple agents are active.
2026-03-13 follow-up after bd 0.60.0 update: the doc/tooling refresh work is resolved via ptrebuild-3di. Local beads mirror was refreshed, repo workflow docs were updated, stale bd sync references were removed from the local workflow, and a derived BEADS_QUICKREF.md generator was added so future command drift is less likely. Active bd now resolves to 0.60.0 from C:\Users\cindi\go\bin. Remaining friction not yet resolved: Windows can still leave the old AppData bd.exe locked on disk even after PATH cleanup, and multi-writer Dolt metadata churn still needs observation in real usage.
title: Agent Ops Epic: tracker/tooling friction log

close_reason: Fixed: markModalOpened() in useMessages.js now PATCHes all unread received messages as read server-side on modal open (fire-and-forget, then refresh). Matches static markReceivedMessagesAsRead() behavior exactly. Commit bc7685d.
description:
Source Reference: QA review discovered this session.

Static parity:
- opening the messages modal updates lastReadMessageTime locally and calls markReceivedMessagesAsRead() to PATCH unread received messages server-side
- static read receipts therefore advance automatically when the modal is opened

Current Next.js bug:
- pages/pt-view.js wires modal-open to useMessages.markModalOpened()
- hooks/useMessages.js markModalOpened() only updates localStorage and unreadCount; it does not PATCH received messages read on the server
- Next.js therefore clears the local badge without actually reproducing static read-state behavior

Evidence:
- public/pt_view.html:2224-2227
- public/pt_view.html:2242-2255
- pages/pt-view.js:341-352
- hooks/useMessages.js:54-57
- hooks/useMessages.js:78-80

Impact:
Server-side read state and read_at/read_by_recipient parity diverge when users simply open the modal, even though static treats modal-open as read.
title: Next.js /pt-view messages modal no longer marks received messages read on open

description:
Source Reference: discovered during pt-unj.1 preview verification (2026-03-21).

Bug:
After Codex's pt-unj.1 fix replaced hardcoded isAdmin=true with role-based detection, the NavMenu no longer shows the '📋 Program Editor' link for either therapist (test-therapist@pttracker.app) or admin (cindi@puppyraiser.com) when on /program. Both accounts can still access the editor directly (page renders correctly), but the nav link is hidden.

Evidence:
- test-patient: blocked with 'Therapist or admin access required.' ✓ (correct)
- test-therapist: editor renders, but NavMenu shows only PT Tracker / View History / Coverage Analysis — no Program Editor
- cindi (admin): same — editor renders, NavMenu missing Program Editor link
- The access gate logic is correct; only the NavMenu isAdmin derivation is wrong for /program

Root cause hypothesis:
/program derives isAdmin from the DB role field. Admin user (cindi) has role 'patient' in the DB (she is the sole patient). Therapist has role 'therapist'. isAdmin check may be using role === 'admin' which matches neither. The correct check should be role === 'therapist' || hasAdminPrivilege.

File scope:
pt-rebuild/pages/program.js
title: PT program page NavMenu missing Program Editor link for therapist and admin after pt-unj.1 isAdmin fix

Issue:
Review and reorganize `pt-rebuild/docs` so current operational docs are easy to find, stale guidance is corrected, generated and legacy files are clearly separated, and historical one-off memos stop competing with living references.

Context:
A review of `pt-rebuild/docs` on 2026-03-20 found several doc-health problems that now make the folder harder to trust and navigate:
- `NEXTJS_MIGRATION.md` mixes historical decisions, live migration status, branch strategy, and architecture rules that now overlap with `README.md` and `NEXTJS_STRUCTURE.md`; it also contradicts itself by saying all work stays on `nextjs` until full completion while also saying incremental merges to `main` are the active strategy.
- `DEVELOPMENT.md` still describes the app as a static frontend in `public/`, which no longer matches the live repo shape documented in `README.md`.
- `TESTING_CHECKLISTS.md` still says the offline log path queues to `localStorage`, while `README.md` now documents IndexedDB-backed offline storage.
- The top-level `docs` folder mixes living references with generated files (`BEADS_QUICKREF.md`, `DEV_NOTES.md`), legacy archive inputs (`dev_notes.json`), and dated one-off memos (`API_SLOT_STRATEGY_2026-02-17.md`, `PHASE_1_ESTIMATE.md`, `SOUND_TRIGGER_AUDIT.md`) without clear grouping or retention rules.
- `AI_WORKFLOW.md` is now essentially a legacy-transition note; its placement and naming make it easy to mistake for an active workflow doc.

Recommended outcome:
- Define a clearer docs information architecture for current references vs generated files vs legacy archive vs dated analysis memos.
- Update current docs so they reflect the live Next.js-plus-legacy hybrid app accurately.
- Reduce duplicate ownership/status guidance across `README.md`, `NEXTJS_MIGRATION.md`, and `NEXTJS_STRUCTURE.md`.
- Add explicit placement/retention rules so future one-off audits and dated memos do not accumulate at the docs root without a home.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Preserve useful historical context; do not delete dated analysis blindly.
- Keep generated files generated, not hand-maintained.
- Respect existing references from `AGENTS.md` and `README.md`; update those references in the same change if files move or are renamed.
- Do not revive `dev_notes` as the active tracker.
- Prefer a small number of clearly named folders or index sections over a deep hierarchy.

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Current-state docs no longer contradict the live repo architecture or offline storage model
- [ ] `README.md`, `NEXTJS_MIGRATION.md`, and `NEXTJS_STRUCTURE.md` have clearly separated roles with minimal duplicated guidance
- [ ] Generated files and legacy archive files are clearly labeled and, if moved, all references are updated
- [ ] Historical memos/audits have an intentional home and naming pattern
- [ ] A future agent can tell where to look for current workflow guidance in under a minute

Dependencies:
- discovered-from: n/a
- blocks: n/a

Reactivation Trigger:

description:
Source DN: DN-050
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: low
Agent: codex

Issue:
Phase 4f: Add exercise sort mode selector + drag-and-drop manual ordering to ExercisePicker. Sort modes: custom/manual, body area, recent activity, alphabetical, PT/program order fallback. Persist preference to localStorage (user-scoped key). Drag-and-drop: pointer events, onPointerUp, 44px grip handle, drag ghost while moving.

File scope:
pt-rebuild/components/ExercisePicker.js, pt-rebuild/components/ExercisePicker.module.css

Context:
Part of Phase 4 index.html migration (DN-045). Resolves DN-012. Sort preference key: pt_sort_mode_${userId}. Custom order key: pt_exercise_order_${userId}. No onclick/click handlers — iOS-safe only. Claude reviews and fixes if needed, then verifies on iOS preview.

Constraints/Caveats:
No onClick or click events. onPointerUp only. touch-action: manipulation. Drag ghost must be visible during move. localStorage keys must include userId to be user-scoped.
notes:
2026-03-21 implementation in progress: added a dedicated tracker sort-state hook plus pure exercise-order helpers so user-scoped sort mode and manual order persist outside pages/index.js. ExercisePicker now owns the visible manual-order UI with a six-dot grip handle, drag ghost, and pointer-driven reordering for manual mode. Local next build passed before the final grip-style polish; preview verification still pending.
2026-03-21 follow-up fix: initial preview test exposed an iOS-style drag lifecycle gap where the ghost could enter a stuck state and background content could still receive interaction. Updated ExercisePicker so manual reorder enters full drag mode immediately, locks background touch interaction during drag, routes move/release through the drag overlay plus window fallback, and cancels back to the original order when release happens outside the list bounds. Local next build passed after this fix; fresh preview retest is next.
2026-03-21 follow-up fix: manual reorder now requires a touch-and-hold on the six-dot grip before drag mode activates. Moving before the hold delay cancels the reorder attempt so normal scrolling does not accidentally pick items up. Preview retest pending after deploy.
2026-03-21 persistence follow-up: manual reorder was not surviving reopen because the stored order could be overwritten before the user-scoped sort state finished hydrating. Scoped fix is in useExerciseSortState so localStorage writes wait until the current user's stored values have been read first. Preview retest pending after deploy.
2026-03-21 reopen persistence root cause confirmed in live preview: the manual-order key was restoring correctly, but the hook rewrote the default order over it during reload before restored state had fully settled. Updated useExerciseSortState so write-back stays disabled until the live sort mode and normalized manual order match the restored user-scoped values.
2026-03-21 second reopen fix: live retest showed hydration was still arming too early while exercises were temporarily empty, which let the default order overwrite the stored manual order before data bootstrap completed. Updated useExerciseSortState so hydrate/write-back stay blocked until the exercise list is populated for the current user.
2026-03-21 restore bug root cause fixed: useExerciseSortState was normalizing manualOrderIds against an empty exercise list on reload, which collapsed the restored saved order to [] before real data arrived. The normalization step now stays off until exercises are loaded, so restored ids survive long enough to be applied.
2026-03-21 final restore race fix in progress: live React inspection showed localStorage still held the custom order, but useExerciseSortState was feeding ExercisePicker a default-order manualOrderIds array because the normalization effect ran during the same mount cycle as the restore effect and won the race. Added a hydration gate so normalization cannot run until the current user's restored state is settled.
2026-03-21 preview verification after ad5bb6f: confirmed restored manual order now aligns across user-scoped localStorage, useExerciseSortState manualOrderIds, and rendered ExercisePicker cards after reload. Live preview came back in the saved custom order (Figure-four stretch, Seated Thoracic Rotation, Abdominal Hollow with Marching, Long Arc Quad, Ball Squeeze, ...), proving the restore path is no longer snapping back to default order during hydration. A synthetic desktop drag attempt in Playwright did not change the order further, so remaining validation is real-device iOS touch reorder + reopen persistence.

Drag-and-drop: pointer events, onPointerUp, 44px grip handle, drag ghost while moving.

client_mutation_id contract is one ID per log submission payload (confirmed 2026-03-03). No onClick. Pattern modifiers: hold_seconds, duration_seconds, distance_feet, side. See AGENTS.md Activity Log Testing Checklist for required test matrix.
notes:
2026-03-06: Added openCreateWithSeedSet() in useSessionLogging to support TimerPanel -> SessionLoggerModal handoff with prefilled first set values while preserving existing create/edit/idempotency flows. Build passes locally. Commit: 46ef84c.
2026-03-13: Codex tightened the TimerPanel -> SessionLoggerModal handoff so app-recorded timer/counter values seed the form with manual_log=false instead of looking like manual entry. Local next build passes after the change.
2026-03-13: Production parity check on https://pttracker.app also clarifies the 4c handoff semantics. From the in-panel exercise logger, 'Log Set' is the manual-entry path and 'Next Set' is the app-recorded path; exercise tap itself does not jump straight into SessionLoggerModal. On production, the logger panel owns the exercise execution flow first, and logging choices happen from within that panel. Current Next.js remains a partial handoff implementation because TimerPanel still exposes 'Use This Value' / 'Open Full Log Form' rather than the production 'Log Set' / 'Next Set' paths. Keep ptrebuild-cva blocked until this panel-level handoff is aligned.
2026-03-13: Production check on https://pttracker.app clarified the 4c handoff semantics. Exercise tap does not open SessionLoggerModal directly. The in-panel logger owns the exercise execution flow first, then exposes two logging paths from inside the panel: 'Log Set' for manual entry and 'Next Set' for the app-recorded path. Keep ptrebuild-cva blocked until Next.js matches that panel-first flow.
2026-03-13: Next.js branch now keeps exercise tap panel-first instead of opening SessionLoggerModal directly, which restores the 4c/4d handoff direction confirmed on production.
2026-03-13 parity review findings still open in 4c: (1) Log Set manual-entry path drops the currently selected side for sided exercises; static carries the in-panel side into manual entry instead of resetting to the default side. (2) Side handling inside the panel-first flow is still incomplete because current Next.js panel state does not yet model session-backed side progress the way static loggerView/currentSession does. These findings are review evidence against the active 4c item, not a separate child issue yet.
2026-03-13: Closed child pt-019 after preview verification on https://pt-rehab-git-nextjs-pt-tracker.vercel.app. Verified current panel side carries into Log Set for sided exercises and panel rows now reflect session-backed left/right progress instead of placeholder counts. ptrebuild-weq remains open because child ptrebuild-1qq (historical form-parameter dropdown parity) is still open.

description:
Source Reference: follow-up design request from user on 2026-03-17
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: low
Scope: current
Agent Owner: codex
Agent Eligible: codex,claude
Tags: nextjs,forms,input,normalization,refactor,ui
File Scope:
- pt-rebuild/components/
- pt-rebuild/lib/
- pt-rebuild/pages/program.js
- pt-rebuild/pages/index.js

Issue:
Design and implement a shared input-normalization mechanism so reusable form fields can explicitly choose how free-text input should be normalized.

Context:
This came up while defining the shared select-plus-`Other...` component/module. Some fields may allow free-text entry and need a consistent normalization rule, such as lowercase, title case, sentence case, or no normalization. That rule should not be reimplemented ad hoc inside each page or field.

Required Direction:
- Add a shared mechanism that lets a field declare its normalization mode.
- Expected modes include at least:
  - `none`
  - `lower`
  - `title`
  - `sentence`
- This should be designed as a separate concern from dropdown interaction behavior, but it must be easy to use together with shared select/`Other...` components.
- Prefer a small shared helper/module plus a clear component API over page-local string manipulation.

Examples:
- A select-with-`Other...` field may allow `Other...` and normalize entered text to lowercase.
- Another field may allow free text but preserve title case.
- A plain text field may opt into the same normalization mechanism without using a dropdown at all.

Constraints/Caveats:
Do not silently force one normalization rule on all fields. The call site must explicitly choose the mode. Preserve the ability for some fields to opt out entirely.

Validation Checklist:
- [ ] Shared normalization mechanism exists
- [ ] Field/API surface allows explicit per-field normalization choice
- [ ] Works cleanly with shared select/`Other...` field behavior
- [ ] Fields can opt out with `none`

Dependencies:
- related: pt-ant

Reactivation Trigger:
n/a
title: Add shared input normalization modes for reusable fields

description: User wants to explore: 1) What custom types make sense for this project (verification, agent-message, etc). 2) Whether types.custom can be used to enforce browser_verify close criteria. 3) Whether bd mail / on_close hooks could be used for cross-agent notification when a bead closes. Review docs/messaging.md in PT_Backup beads clone — bd mail is a first-class feature using BEADS_ACTOR identity. Hook system (on_create, on_update, on_close) receives JSON on stdin — could be used to enforce status rules at close time. Explore with user before implementing anything.
notes:
Docs to read next session:
- PT_Backup/beads/docs/CONFIG.md — types.custom section (custom issue types)
- PT_Backup/beads/docs/CLI_REFERENCE.md — bd types command, custom types examples
- PT_Backup/beads/docs/messaging.md — bd mail setup (already read, notes below)
- PT_Backup/beads/docs/PLUGIN.md — on_close hook details for enforcement

bd mail setup questions to investigate:
- messaging.md says bd mail delegates to an external provider via BEADS_MAIL_DELEGATE env var (e.g. 'gt mail' in an orchestrator context)
- 'gt mail' appears to be Gas Town / orchestrator-specific — may not apply here
- Need to check: is there a standalone bd mail setup that works without an orchestrator?
- Or can we use on_close hook directly for cross-agent notification instead of bd mail?
- BEADS_ACTOR env var sets sender identity (replaces deprecated BD_ACTOR)
- Check if bd mail works standalone: bd mail inbox / bd mail send — try with just BEADS_ACTOR set
bd mail requires Gas Town orchestrator — too much overhead, not applicable here. Cross-agent notification path: on_close hook writes bd note to other agent's AMC bead directly. Zero external dependencies. Pursue this instead of bd mail.
claude-handoff = no. Claude Code desktop only, not CLI, and doesn't help Codex anyway. beads-compound = no, Codex not supported. BeadHub = worth investigating for inter-agent messaging — agent agnostic bd wrapper, has async mail + sync chat. Check version compatibility with bd 0.62.0 first. Primary path remains on_close hook (fires for both agents). Secondary: BeadHub if version-compatible.
Community tools investigation complete.

WORTH PURSUING:
- BeadHub (bdh) — explicit Claude Code + Codex support. File locking prevents agent collisions on shared files. Async mail works for both. Codex limitation: cannot self-notify, human must prompt. Docker or hosted at beadhub.ai (free for OSS). Check 0.62.0 compat.
- Foolery — dispatches + monitors both agents from one UI. Supports Claude Code + Codex CLI explicitly. Routes task types per agent. Local server, Node.js only. v0.4.0 (2026-03-11), actively maintained.
- beads-web — human Kanban visibility layer. Windows x64 binary. Dolt-aware. beads-ui is lighter alternative (npm install -g).

RULED OUT: claude-handoff (Claude only), claude-protocol (hooks Claude only), beads-sdk (dev lib), lavra/beads-compound (no Codex).

CUSTOM COMMAND IDEAS from reviewing these tools:
1. /session-start — read + close previous AMC bead, create new one, run bd ready filtered by assignee. Replaces manual session startup ritual.
2. /session-end — bd list in_progress sweep, close done beads, final bd note on AMC bead, git push, bd dolt push. Replaces manual landing-the-plane.
3. /handoff — write structured context note to other agent's AMC bead via bd note. Lightweight version of claude-handoff without the file complexity.
4. /verify-queue — list all browser_verify beads assigned to claude, open preview URL. One command to start a verification pass.
5. on_close hook — check if closing bead was browser_verify, require verification evidence in close reason (enforcement not just convention).
6. file-lock pattern from BeadHub — before touching a shared file, check if other agent has it claimed. Could be a pre-coding step in AGENTS.md even without BeadHub installed.
BeadHub clarification: does NOT replace beads. bdh is a transparent wrapper over bd — calls bd commands under the hood. Existing beads database, Dolt sync, all beads untouched. Additive only. User runs Docker so server requirement is not a blocker.

FINAL INVESTIGATION CRITERIA before adopting BeadHub or Foolery:
1. Resource usage — how much CPU/RAM does the server consume at idle and under load? (Serena was rejected for this reason — too heavy)
2. MCP or not? — if it adds an MCP server, that means +++ tokens per tool call. Need to know if bdh/Foolery use MCP or pure CLI.
3. Token impact — does it add tokens to every message (like MCP system prompts do), or is it CLI-only (zero token overhead when not actively called)? Net token cost vs. any savings from better coordination.

Serena precedent: rejected because resource/token overhead wasn't worth the benefit. Apply same bar here.
CRITICAL resource bar: Serena caused a full machine lockup — not just slow, machine became unusable. User's laptop is older. This is a hard requirement, not a preference. BeadHub/Foolery must be confirmed lightweight before any recommendation to install.
title: Explore custom issue types and browser_verify enforcement

description: Source Reference: child of pt-am6 created from tracker hygiene discussion on 2026-03-17. Produce an independent Codex proposal for how Beads assignments should work when agents discover new issues. Problem: the discovering agent often tags the issue to itself, which means the other agent may never even see it while looking for work. Review the current workflow and propose concrete rules for: when discovered issues should stay assigned to the finder, when they should be left unassigned, when they should move into a shared pool, and whether a third shared/unassigned category should be used for work either Codex or Claude can do. Focus on practical rules that improve visibility without creating chaos.
title: Codex pass: revise assignment rules for discovered work

close_reason: Completed: distance dosage saves now match static payload shape, update validation no longer rejects hidden reps, and helper tests pass.
description:
## Summary
Exercises that use the distance_feet dosage shape cannot be configured in the program editor because saving dosage fails with the validation error eps_per_set must be a positive number, even though the visible inputs only ask for sets and distance.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Open /program and select or create an exercise that uses the distance_feet dosage shape.
2. Enter values in the visible dosage fields for sets and distance.
3. Attempt to save the dosage.
4. Observe the error eps_per_set must be a positive number.

## Context
This appears to be a dosage-model mismatch between the UI for distance-based exercises and the validation or payload mapping path that still expects eps_per_set.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Reproduce the failure on an exercise that uses distance_feet
- Identify whether the validator, mapping layer, or form payload incorrectly requires eps_per_set
- Clarify the intended valid dosage shape for distance-based exercises

## Notes
User asked that this be treated as P1 because it prevents use of the app for affected exercises. No code changes yet.
notes:
Root cause confirmed: Next.js DosageModal sent hidden distance/duration fields as explicit nulls, while legacy static pt_editor omitted reps_per_set entirely for distance saves. The update handler treated reps_per_set:null as invalid before considering dosage_type=distance. Verified local backup file C:\Users\cindi\OneDrive\Documents\PT_Backup\db-backups\backup-2026-03-23_03-48-51.json shows a real distance program row with dosage_type=distance and a persisted reps_per_set value, so the fix preserves existing stored reps instead of clearing them.
Root cause: React DosageModal sent hidden distance fields as explicit nulls, but static pt_editor omitted reps_per_set entirely for distance saves. Backup check in PT_Backup/db-backups/backup-2026-03-23_03-48-51.json confirmed a real distance program row can keep a stored reps_per_set value, so the fix preserves existing reps and stops the null-validation error.
title: Dosage save fails for distance_feet exercises

close_reason: Closed
description:
Source Reference: none (discovered from ptrebuild-kj5)
Status: open
Priority: P1
Risk: medium
Scope: Next.js offline read path
Agent Owner: claude
Agent Eligible: yes
Tags: offline, cache, exercises, reliability, nextjs, migration, indexeddb
File Scope: pt-rebuild/hooks/useIndexData.js, pt-rebuild/lib/index-cache.js (new)

Issue:
When the app is opened without a network connection, exercises, programs, and logs fail to load. There is no read-side IndexedDB cache in the Next.js version. User sees 'Offline - no cached version available' (service worker level) or empty state.

Root Cause (Static App):
The static app's service worker caches '/index.html' during install but browsers navigate to '/' (root URL) — different cache keys. The dynamic cache.put() for '/' from network-first visits does not persist reliably on iOS between sessions. So on offline cold-open, the page itself fails to load before any JS or IndexedDB code runs.

Additionally, hydrateCache() in offline.js is called fire-and-forget AFTER exercises render, making additional network requests. On iOS this approach is unreliable.

Root Cause (Next.js):
No read-side cache at all. useIndexData.js fetches from API only — no fallback.

Context:
Static app uses IndexedDB via offline.js (OfflineManager class). IndexedDB was chosen deliberately over localStorage for storage size, non-blocking async I/O, and transactional writes. This decision stands for Next.js.

Key improvement over static app: in Next.js, data is already in memory after the Promise.all() fetch succeeds. We write to IndexedDB immediately from in-memory data — no second network round trip. Cache write is guaranteed before setExercises/setPrograms/setLogs update state.

Constraints/Caveats:
- Use IndexedDB (consistent with static app design decision, more reliable than localStorage for read cache).
- All IndexedDB access must be inside useEffect/useCallback (never during SSR). Guard with typeof window !== 'undefined'.
- DB name: pt_tracker_offline (same as static app — shares the already-hydrated cache if user switches between static and Next.js during migration).
- Cache is read-only (write after fetch, read as fallback). Write queue stays in localStorage (pt_offline_queue_${userId}).
- Clear cache on sign-out to prevent cross-user leakage.
- Do NOT re-introduce /api/sync.
- SSR guard required: all IndexedDB calls must be client-side only.

Specific Fix:
1. lib/index-cache.js (new file — mirrors offline.js OfflineManager pattern for Next.js):
   - IndexCacheManager class with init(), hydrateFromData(exercises, programs, logs), getCachedExercises(), getCachedPrograms(), getCachedLogs(), clearCache()
   - hydrateFromData() takes already-fetched data (no network calls) — writes to IndexedDB transactionally
   - DB name: pt_tracker_offline, DB version: 2 (same stores: exercises, programs, activity_logs)
   - Export singleton: export const indexCache = new IndexCacheManager()

2. hooks/useIndexData.js — modify reload():
   - On mount: call indexCache.init() (client-side only, inside useEffect)
   - After successful Promise.all fetch: call indexCache.hydrateFromData(exercises, programs, logs) — synchronous write before setExercises
   - In catch block: attempt indexCache.getCachedExercises/Programs/Logs(); if any non-empty, use cached data; expose fromCache: boolean
   - On sign-out (userId becomes null): call indexCache.clearCache()

Validation Checklist:
- Open app online → data loads from API → IndexedDB written immediately
- Open app offline (cache exists) → data loads from IndexedDB → no error
- Open app offline (cache empty, first use) → error shown: offline, no cached data
- Sign out → IndexedDB cache cleared
- Sign back in → fresh API fetch, cache updated
- No SSR errors (typeof window guard)

Dependencies: discovered-from ptrebuild-kj5, child of ptrebuild-nyn

Note: The service worker cache key bug (/ vs /index.html) is tracked separately in dev_notes.json (static app fix). Next.js PWA service worker fix is in ptrebuild-v3l — see constraint added there.

Closure Gate:
- Do not close this implementation bead until the linked parity bead (pt-780.1) is verified or closed.
- This bead covers implementation of offline read-cache behavior; pt-780.1 remains the final acceptance surface for offline startup and cache-fallback parity.
notes:
2026-03-13 QA evidence: confirmed from source. hooks/useIndexData.js has no read-side cache path at all: reload() only does Promise.all network fetches at hooks/useIndexData.js:12-24, then sets error on failure at hooks/useIndexData.js:25-27. When token/patientId are absent it simply clears in-memory state at hooks/useIndexData.js:33-40. lib/index-offline.js currently implements only the queue helpers (queueKey/loadQueue/saveQueue/clearQueue) at lib/index-offline.js:10-44 plus payload shaping; there are no data-cache helpers for exercises/programs/logs. pages/index.js consumes useIndexData directly at pages/index.js:29 and surfaces the resulting load error banner at pages/index.js:223-224. Sign-out clears only the offline queue via clearQueue() in pages/index.js:174-177; there is currently no corresponding cached-read data lifecycle. This bead is accurate as written.
2026-03-21 implementation in progress: wiring tracker bootstrap into the shared offlineCache helper instead of introducing a parallel cache layer. Current changes add IndexedDB-backed cache writes/fallback to hooks/useIndexData.js after successful exercises/programs/logs fetches, add targeted clearPrograms/clearExercises/clearLogs helpers to lib/offline-cache.js for sign-out cleanup, and update README ownership notes to document tracker read-side fallback on the shared cache path.
2026-03-21 landed implementation on nextjs in commit fd8ac79. hooks/useIndexData.js now writes exercises/programs/logs into the shared offlineCache after successful fetches, falls back to cached tracker bootstrap data when network fetches fail, exposes fromCache for downstream offline-state UI, and clears tracker read-side stores when auth drops to avoid cross-user leakage. lib/offline-cache.js now includes targeted clearExercises/clearPrograms/clearLogs helpers, and README ownership notes were updated to reflect tracker use of the shared IndexedDB cache.
2026-03-21 preview check after push: deployed preview loaded successfully, but the fresh isolated Chrome DevTools context did not have a saved authenticated session, so signed-in tracker bootstrap fallback could not be exercised end-to-end in this pass. Remaining runtime verification needed: warm the tracker online while signed in, confirm exercises/programs/logs are written to IndexedDB, then switch offline and verify the tracker hydrates from cache instead of showing the load-error state. Do not close pt-zb3 until that authenticated cache-fallback check is complete and pt-780.1 acceptance is handled.
2026-03-21 follow-up implementation: added tracker shell feedback for cache fallback and aligned failed-load copy with the parity spec. pages/index.js now renders a visible 'Using cached data' banner when useIndexData reports fromCache, hooks/useIndexData.js normalizes the no-cache exercise failure to 'Failed to load exercises. Check your connection.' and log failure to 'Failed to load history.', and pages/index.module.css / README were updated to document the new shell feedback path.
2026-03-21 follow-up implementation committed locally in c9f90d7: tracker read-side fallback now uses a patient-scoped bootstrap snapshot in offline-cache/useIndexData instead of any generic cached exercises/programs/logs. This avoids applying cached tracker data from the wrong resolved patient context during offline fallback. Not yet pushed or preview-verified.
title: Offline cold-open: exercises, programs, and logs do not load when app is opened without network

description:
Source Reference: discovered during Playwright parity verification of pt-spg.4 on 2026-03-22.
Status: open
Priority: P2
Risk: medium
Scope: current

Tags: nextjs,migration,parity,logging,side-switch,logger
File Scope:

- pt-rebuild/components/SessionLoggerModal.js

Issue:
When logging a sided exercise through Log Set, the tracker does not preserve the currently selected side after a save. In Playwright preview verification, Long Arc Quad was logged once on right, then the main tracker side was switched to left, Log Set opened with Side=Left as expected, and saving that left-side set returned to the panel showing Working right side instead of staying on left. The left set was recorded (left count incremented), but active side context snapped back to right.
Context:
This was found while verifying pt-spg.4 against the deployed nextjs preview. Existing side behavior that does work: side controls render; switching to left updates the panel label; Log Set modal inherits the current tracker side; manual log saves use modal-local side for the saved set. The remaining bug is persistence of active side context after the modal save completes.
Constraints/Caveats:
Do not change bilateral null-side semantics while fixing this. Preserve the existing correct modal-local side save behavior.
Validation Checklist:
- [ ] After saving a left-side Log Set, the panel remains on left
- [ ] After saving a right-side Log Set, the panel remains on right
- [ ] Saved set side still comes from modal-local side selection
- [ ] Bilateral exercises still keep null side semantics
Dependencies:
- discovered-from: pt-spg.4

n/a
title: Keep active side on selected side after Log Set save
